Folding is the most disciplined and profitable play in the long run rather than floating out of position.

6s3h is a very weak hand, but in a 3-handed game on the button I should open a wide range to steal the blinds. With pot 150 and To Call 100 (min-raise to 200), I’ll make a standard 3xBB open to 300 — big enough to generate fold equity and to avoid inviting cheap multiway pots or easy 3-bets. If I get called, I’ll use position to play cautiously postflop and c-bet favorable textures; if faced with a large 3-bet I’ll fold to preserve my stack given the hand's poor equity.

In a 3-handed game, Qd 8d is a strong enough suited hand to defend the Small Blind against a standard Button open of 3x. The pot odds are favorable (250 to win 450, approximately 2.8:1), and the hand has decent playability post-flop. While 3-betting is an option, calling allows me to see a flop efficiently without bloating the pot out of position against a potentially wide range from Minni.

65s is a speculative suited connector with good playability postflop. Facing a button raise to 300 and SB call, pot is 700, and I need to call 200 more from the BB (total 300 committed). This gives excellent pot odds of about 3.5:1. Multiway pots favor speculative hands like this due to implied odds and position postflop (acting before button). Opponents' ranges are wide: Minni button raise can be light, Flash SB call also wide. Folding is too tight deepstacked; raising is overplaying. Standard defend by calling to realize equity.

I am first to act on a dry flop of 5d Ts 2h with Queen high and a backdoor flush draw. As the caller out of position, the standard play is to check to the pre-flop aggressor (Minni). I don't have enough equity to lead into two opponents, so I will check and likely fold if there is significant betting action.

Middle pair with a mediocre 6 kicker on a relatively dry rainbow board (5d-Ts-2h). Flash's SB check suggests weakness, as they defend wide preflop but check weak hands first in. Multiway pot deepstacked, checking promotes pot control, induces bluffs from Minni (button raiser with wide range), and realizes equity cheaply on favorable turn cards (e.g., 3/4/7 for straights or heart). Thin value betting risks folding out better/raising worse, and vulnerable to button aggression. Standard check to the preflop raiser.

I was the preflop aggressor in a three-way pot and this flop (5d Ts 2h) is very dry. My 6s3h has only a 4-out gutshot (and minimal showdown value), so my line should be to try to win the pot now with fold equity rather than passively give up. I’ll make a half-pot continuation bet (~450 into 900) — large enough to fold out A-highs and weaker broadways while keeping my commitment reasonable if called. If I face a check-raise I’ll fold; if called I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and only continue barreling with real improvement or strong reason to bluff further.

I have Queen-high with a weak backdoor flush draw on a dry board of Ten-Five-Deuce. Minni has made a standard half-pot continuation bet into two opponents. Since I have no pair and no real draw, and Elon is still to act behind me, calling is unprofitable. I do not have enough equity to continue in this hand.

Middle pair with 6 kicker on a dry rainbow flop (5d-Ts-2h). Facing Minni's button continuation bet of 450 into 1350 pot after two checks (Flash folded), getting excellent 3:1 pot odds requiring only ~25% equity. My hand has decent showdown value, blocks some straight draws (e.g., 34, 36), and suited connector-like playability for turns that improve (3/4/7/heart). Minni's button raise + c-bet range is wide and polarized: bluffs (missed Ax, broadways, gutshots), thin value (Tx, overcards), and strong hands (sets, two-pair). Folding is too tight OOP deepstacked with position lost but implied odds intact; raising thin value risks folding out worse while getting snapped by better. Calling realizes equity cheaply, controls pot size, induces bluffs on blank turns, and sets up check-raises or folds to aggression if board worsens. Consistent with prior flop check for pot control. History shows Minni aggressive but foldy to pressure (e.g., Hand 13).

On the turn, board is 5d-Ts-2h-Td, giving me bottom pair (5s) with a 6 kicker. Acting first OOP in a 1800 pot after checking flop and calling Minni's 450 c-bet. The paired Tens improve villain's range significantly (many Tx, two-pair combos like T5/T2), while my hand remains marginal with showdown value but vulnerable to any aggression. Few strong draws remain (diamond flush draw now has two to hit, heart draw brick, straight draws limited). Betting risks folding out worse hands (Ax, weak pairs, air) while getting raised/folded on by better (TT, T5, sets). Checking maintains pot control, induces bluffs/check-behinds from villain's wide button range (Ax high cards, missed draws, weak Tx that fear better), and allows cheap realization of equity. Consistent with flop check/call strategy for speculative BB defense OOP deepstacked (~100BB). History shows Minni aggressive but capable of checking/showdown (e.g., Hand 11,17). Will evaluate river based on action.

I was the preflop aggressor and c-bet the dry flop, but the turn (Td) pairs the board and strongly helps Elon's calling range (he could have Tx -> now trips, 5x -> now two pair, or pocket pairs). I hold 6♠3♥ with no showdown value and no real improvement. Elon checked the turn, which looks like pot-control with a medium hand or a trap with a very strong hand — either way, barreling again is thin and will often run into hands that now beat me. Pot control and preserving my stack is the best line: check the turn, take a free river card, and reassess. If Elon barrels the river I’ll fold to large sizing and only call small bets with some caution; I’ll only bluff the river with a very clear read or a favorable runout.

River pairs the 5s, improving me to trip 5s (5h with board 5d/5s) with 6c kicker on board 5d-Ts-2h-Td-5s. This is a very strong hand OOP heads-up vs Minni's button range after her flop c-bet (Tx, 5x, Ax, draws, two-pair like T2/T5) and turn check-behind (suggests medium strength like single T, 5x, or missed draws rather than monsters). Beats most of her value range (top pair Tx with weak kickers, bottom pair 5x worse kicker, sets unlikely without prior action), loses mainly to specific full houses (T5 combos) or quads (pocket TT, very narrow). Pot 1800 deepstacked (~100BB), betting extracts value from worse pairs/draws/Ax that showdown/call, denies equity to rivers that beat us (unlikely outs left), and gets called by ~1/3 pot worth. Sizing 1200 (~2/3 pot) balances value/thin value while protecting vs unlikely better. Checking risks free showdown (loses to Tx/Ax) or missed value if she bluffs; betting consistent with occasional strong BB defenses realizing massive equity payoff here. Minni history shows calling stations sometimes (e.g., Hand 6) but aggressive overall.

Elon’s river lead strongly represents value. He called my flop c-bet, checked the turn when the Ten paired (either pot-control with a strong hand or a trap), and now fires 1,200 into a 1,800 pot — the current pot is 3,000 after his bet. The board (Ts Td 5d 5s 2h) is double-paired, so any Ten or Five in his hand becomes a full house (and pocket Tens/Fives/quads are also possible). Calling 1,200 requires ~28.6% equity (1,200 / 4,200), which 6♠3♥ does not have versus a value-polarized range; my hand only beats rare bluffs or ties to the board. Raising as a bluff here is too risky (no meaningful blockers and a large sizing). Long-term stack preservation and pot control dictate folding to this river bet.

I am on the button with 83o. This is a very weak starting hand even in a three-handed game. While button aggression is important, 83o does not have enough equity or playability to justify a raise or a call. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

AhJc is a strong top-tier hand from the small blind after the button has folded. It plays well post-flop in position against the big blind and has good equity realization. Standard play is to raise to isolate and build the pot with this premium holding. Raising to 3x the big blind (300 total) is a balanced size that fits my opening range and applies pressure on Minni, who has shown passive tendencies in recent hands. No reason to limp or fold such a strong hand here with deep stacks.

I’m in the big blind with 3♦6♠ facing a small‑blind open to 300. The pot is 400 and it’s 200 to call (about 33% pot odds). This hand has very poor playability and will be dominated a large portion of the time versus a SB opening range. I’ll be out of position postflop, have weak equity and poor implied odds, and 3-betting as a bluff here is thin with 100+bb stacks. I defend this spot with suited connectors, small pairs and some A‑x, but 3♦6♠ is not one of them. Fold and preserve stack for better, higher-expected-value spots.

3-handed on the button with deep stacks. 5h3d is a weak offsuit hand but has some wheel straight potential and blockers to low straights. Facing only the blinds with pot odds of roughly 1.5:1 to call, but raising has high fold equity against likely passive defenses from Minni and Flash based on history. They often fold to button opens. Standard play is to open-raise wide from button in short-handed games to steal blinds and apply pressure. Raising to 3x BB (300) builds pot in position or takes it down immediately.

I’m in the small blind with A♣7♦ facing a button open to 300. This is a playable ace but with a weak kicker and I’ll be out of position postflop; the big blind can still come along and make this multiway. I’d rather realize equity and keep the pot manageable than 3-bet OOP with a dominated hand (a min-raise to 500 won’t credibly isolate and a larger 3-bet commits chips without positional advantage). Call 250 more (300 total) to see a flop, fold to a big 3-bet preflop, and proceed cautiously on the flop — look to extract value when I cleanly hit an ace, otherwise check/fold to heavy pressure or continue only with strong draws/top pair-clean boards.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-King of hearts, a premium starting hand. Elon (Button) has raised to 300, and Minni (Small Blind) has called. This is a prime opportunity for a squeeze play. 3-betting for value is essential here to build a pot and ideally take control of the hand out of position or win the pot pre-flop. A raise to 1300 is a standard sizing for a squeeze from the blinds against an opener and a caller, putting significant pressure on their likely wider ranges.

3-handed on the button with 5h3d, a speculative offsuit hand with poor postflop playability. Opened wide preflop as standard. Now facing a large 3-bet to 1300 from Flash in BB after Minni's SB call, making pot 1900 and requiring 1000 to call (pot odds ~2:1, needing ~33% equity). 53o has weak equity (~20-25%) against typical BB 3-bet range (TT+, AQ+, some bluffs), even worse multiway with Minni in. No strong blockers or combo potential to justify a 4-bet bluff. Deep stacks favor folding trash here, preserving stack for better positions and continued button pressure. History shows opponents fold often to aggression, so reload next orbit.

I’m OOP in the small blind with A♣7♦. I limp-called the button’s 300 open and the BB (Flash) 3‑bet to 1300; Elon folded so it’s heads‑up. A7o has a weak kicker and is frequently dominated by a typical 3‑betting range (pairs, stronger Ax, broadways). Pot is 1900 and I must call 1000 (need ~34% equity) but have poor postflop playability OOP and limited implied odds. A 4‑bet bluff to 2300 would bloat the pot out of position against a player who shows aggression and likely calls with better hands. I planned to fold to a sizable 3‑bet preflop, so I’ll preserve my stack for better, positionally advantaged spots and fold now.

I'm on the button with Qc2c — a weak but suited hand. Versus only the blinds this is squarely in my opening range: position + suitedness + fold equity make it profitable to raise rather than limp. I'll open to a standard 3x BB (300) to apply pressure and define ranges. Postflop plan: if I'm called I'll use my positional advantage to c-bet small on dry, uncoordinated flops and to pot-control or check back on dangerous, connected, or Q-high boards where I'm likely dominated. If I face a substantial 3-bet (e.g. ~900+), I will fold Q2s rather than play a dominated spot out of position.
I am in the Small Blind with 6c 3h, which is a very weak hand. Minni raised to 300 from the Button. Folding is the standard and correct play here to avoid playing a weak hand out of position.

Q6o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against Minni's button opening range, which is likely wide but still favors hands that dominate mine. Facing a standard 3x raise after SB folds, pot odds are decent at ~2.25:1 effective, but postflop prospects are poor due to reverse implied odds and difficulty to realize equity. Deep stacks don't help this marginal hand. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

I am on the button with Ten-Seven offsuit. In a three-handed game, opening ranges are wider, but Ten-Seven offsuit is generally just below the threshold of a profitable opening hand from this position. Given that the players in the blinds have shown a willingness to contest pots, I will play conservatively and fold this hand to wait for a better opportunity.

Preflop in SB vs BB after BTN fold. Standard spot to raise wide for fold equity and steal blinds. K2o has King blocker, decent high card, and postflop playability in position if called. Minni has shown tendency to call raises but also folds sometimes (e.g., Hand 13). Raising to 3x (300 total) is standard size for value/steal. Folding too tight, checking gives up initiative with poor postflop hand.

A♠Qc is a premium hand in the Big Blind facing a 3bb open from the Small Blind. I want to take the initiative and isolate Elon while I'm in position postflop (he will act first after the flop), so a standard 3-bet is correct. A raise to 900 (≈3x his open) gives good fold equity versus his opening range, prices out speculative hands, and keeps the pot size and SPR manageable with deep stacks. Plan: if he calls we play postflop in position with a hand that fares well on a lot of boards and will c-bet favorable textures; if he 4-bets big I can fold or re-evaluate based on sizing (fold to large polarized 4-bets, call smaller ones). Action: raise to 900 to extract value and apply pressure.

Facing a 3-bet from Minni in the BB after my light SB open with K2o. Pot is 1200, call requires 600 more (pot odds ~3:1, needing ~25% equity). K2o has poor equity (~20-30%) against a typical BB 3-bet range (JJ+, AKo, AQs+), and even less against tighter ranges. Postflop, I'll be OOP with a marginal hand lacking strong playability or blockers beyond the K. Minni has folded to 3-bets before (e.g., Hand 14, 22) but her 3-bet here suggests strength. 4-betting as a bluff is risky OOP deep-stacked with this holding; fold equity low vs her range. History shows Minni defends/calls wide but 3-bets indicate polarization. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

SB (Minni) folded, BB (Flash) checked, action folded to me on the button pre-flop. Pot 150 with dead money from SB blind. TsJd (JTo offsuit) is well above 3-handed button open-raising range (typically 40-60% of hands). Opponents appear passive based on history (frequent checks and folds). Standard play is to raise for value/steal, sizing to 300 (3x BB), a common pre-flop raise size in prior hands. Limping or folding too weak from this position; post-flop playability good if called.

Fold. 8♠4♠ is too weak to defend out of position against a button open. Calling 250 into a 450 pot requires roughly 36% equity to break even, and this hand has poor showdown and post‑flop playability OOP versus both the opener and the big blind. A tiny 3‑bet bluff is unlikely to fold enough of the button's range and would put me in a dominated, awkward spot. Preserve the 10,700 stack for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Seven offsuit. The Button (Elon) has raised to 300. In a 3-handed game, Button opening ranges are typically wide. I need to call 200 more into a pot that will be 600, giving me 3:1 pot odds (25% equity required). K7o is a standard defending hand in this spot, as it has enough raw equity against a wide range to justify seeing a flop, even though I will be out of position for the rest of the hand.

On a flop of 9c-5d-7h, I have middle pair with a King kicker. This is a solid hand in a blind-versus-button situation. Standard play as the Big Blind is to check to the pre-flop aggressor. If Elon bets, I plan to call because my hand is strong enough to continue against his likely wide range of hands, although the board texture is somewhat coordinated with straight draws (68, 8T).

Flop board 9c-5d-7h is fairly dry and uncoordinated, rainbow, missing my JTo completely—no pair, no strong draw (minor gutshot potential at best with 6x or 8x, but unlikely). As preflop raiser on button vs. passive BB who checked, this is a standard spot for a continuation bet to exploit their wide calling range preflop and likely weak holdings now (Ax, offsuit broadways, small pairs). History shows opponents check/fold frequently, especially Flash. Pot control isn't an issue with position and deep stacks. Sizing to ~60% pot (400 into 650) balances value (if they have weaker pairs/draws) and bluffs, denies equity, and builds pot if ahead. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or inducing bluffs later, but betting maintains initiative.

On a flop of 9c-5d-7h, I have middle pair with a King kicker. Elon is betting 400 into a pot of 650, which is a standard continuation bet from the Button. My hand is well ahead of his air and many of his overcard hands. While the board is somewhat connected (straight draws like 68, 8T), middle pair is a mandatory continuation. I will call to see how the turn develops, as raising might bloat the pot unnecessarily or force out his bluffs.

The turn card (2s) is a total brick that doesn't change the board texture significantly. I still have middle pair with a King kicker, which is a very strong hand in a blind-versus-button encounter. I will continue with the plan of checking to the aggressor to let him continue his bluffs or check back marginal hands. There is no reason to lead here and bloat the pot or fold out his bluffs.

Board is 9c-5d-7h-2s, very dry and uncoordinated. My TsJd has complete air—no pair, no draw, just backdoor potential. Preflop I raised button standard, c-bet flop as continuation which Flash called (likely has showdown value like a weak pair, overcards, or weak draw from wide BB calling range). Flash checks again on turn, which changes little (2s adds minor straight outs but no flush draw). History shows Flash passive: frequent checks and folds to aggression (e.g., Hand 2 flop fold, Hand 16 fold, Hand 19 fold). However, calling flop suggests some strength or stubbornness with medium strength. With position, deep stacks (pot 1450, SPR ~8), checking behind pot-controls my bluff, takes free river card (board unlikely to brick perfectly for me), realizes equity if villain has weaker air/draw, and sets up potential river bluff or check-down. Betting turn risks facing call from pairs (99/77/55/88/66) and getting shut out on river; villain's check doesn't scream weakness enough to warrant a turn bluff (passive players check-call pairs). Checking maintains balance in my wide button range and exploits passivity without over-bluffing into likely medium strength.

On this river board of 9c-5d-7h-2s-Ah, I have second pair with a King kicker. The Ace is a significant scare card. Elon checked back the turn, which suggests he either has a marginal hand (like a 9 or a medium pocket pair) or was giving up on a bluff. By checking, I allow Elon to check back his own marginal hands, which I might beat, or to attempt a bluff with his missed draws (like 68 or JT). Betting doesn't accomplish much as I'm unlikely to be called by worse than a 7, and I could easily be called or raised by a 9 or a slow-played Ace. I will check and see if he wants to take it to showdown or represent the Ace.

River ace completes some straight draws (T8, 86) but blocks strong Ax hands that villain might check-call with. Board remains dry, low coordinated. My TsJd is pure air (J-high), but as preflop raiser/c-bettor who checked turn, my range is polarized: value (sets, two-pair, overpairs) or bluffs (missed broadways, backdoors). Flash's line—wide BB call pre, check-call flop, check-check turn/river—screams medium strength showdown value (88/66, weak Ax, overcards like KQ/KT) or stubborn floats, unlikely monsters given passivity (history: frequent check-folds, e.g., Hands 2,16,19). Checking loses to all these; bluff exploits passivity, denies equity, and river A improves my backdoor spade potential slightly while scaring villain. Deep SPR gone (pot 1450, stacks deep but commitment low), bet ~60% pot (900) sizes for value from villain's Ax/Tx while folding out J-high/A-high/88. History shows Flash folds to river aggression (e.g., Hand 20 you bet river he folded). Checking realizes 0 EV with air vs passive check-downs.

On a board of 9c-5d-7h-2s-Ah, I have a pair of sevens (middle pair that became third pair on the river). Elon raised pre-flop and bet the flop, but checked the turn. The turn check-back often indicates a marginal hand (like a 9 or a 7) or a draw that is taking a free card. The Ace on the river is a classic scare card. While Elon could have checked back an Ace on the turn for pot control or balance, it is also a very likely card for him to bluff on if he missed a straight draw (like 68, 8T, or JT). The pot is 1450 and the bet is 900, requiring me to be correct about 28% of the time. In a three-handed game, ranges are wide, and Elon's turn check makes his range look more polarized towards marginal made hands or missed draws on this river. My pair of sevens is a reasonable bluff catcher in this spot, so I will call.

I will open to 300 (3x the BB). In a 3-handed game the button should be opening wide to steal blinds and take initiative; 2♣3♥ is a very weak hand but has positional value and some small straight/wheel potential. A standard 3x open maximizes fold equity while keeping the pot controllable. Plan: fold to any sizable 3‑bet (this hand plays poorly out of position vs a strong reraise); if called, proceed postflop in position—c‑bet selectively on favorable textures, check/fold to heavy resistance, and realize equity when I hit. Stack depth (~107 BB) allows maneuvering but demands discipline against big aggression.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Four offsuit. Minni opened to 300 from the Button. While King-high has some raw equity, King-Four offsuit plays very poorly out of position and doesn't flop well. Given my significant chip lead, I should avoid high-variance, marginal spots where I'll be forced to play a weak hand out of position for the rest of the hand. Folding here is the most disciplined play to conserve my stack for more advantageous situations.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 after small blind folds. My hand J9o has decent playability, straight potential, and good equity against a wide button opening range. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into a 450 pot (roughly 31% equity needed, but J9o realizes more due to position). Defending wide vs button is standard. No need to fold or 3-bet as it's speculative and postflop position is great. Plan to play postflop aggressively if hits or as float.

On the flop as BB first to act with J9o giving an open-ended straight draw (8 outs: 4x6, 4x10) against a wide button opening range. Board Ad-8h-7d has some coordination (diamond flush draw, gutshots), but my equity is strong (~30-40% vs typical range). Pot 650, deep stacks. Betting as a semi-bluff protects my draw, denies equity to opponent's floats/ weaker draws, and gets value from Ax, overpairs that missed, or weaker made hands. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards or backdoors. Standard size ~60% pot (400) balances value and fold equity. Plan to barrel turn if clean or check-raise if raised.

Elon C‑bet 400 into the pot (pot is 1,050). Calling 400 requires roughly 400/(1,050+400) ≈ 27.6% equity. With 2♣3♥ I only have ~6 outs to pair by the river (≈24% to pair), and even if I pair I’m often behind an Ace or a better pair. His bet on A‑8‑7 heavily favors his range (lots of Ax and high cards), and attempting a bluff‑raise with deep stacks is too risky — he can call or 3‑bet and I have virtually no fold equity or hand equity. Fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

I am on the button with 9-5 offsuit. This is a very weak starting hand, even in a three-handed game. While I have the chip lead and positional advantage, 95o does not play well post-flop and is generally a fold in standard opening ranges. Since Elon and Minni have both been fairly active and willing to defend or 3-bet, there is no need to bloat the pot with a sub-optimal hand.

In the small blind with Jd8d, suited connectors with straight and flush potential, it's a standard raising hand against the big blind after the button folds. Raising takes the initiative, has good implied odds if called, and folding would be too tight while limping is too passive out of position. Standard raise to 300 (3x the big blind) to steal or play profitably post-flop.

I have A♠J♠ in the big blind facing a 3x open to 300 from the small blind. This is a strong hand heads-up with good playability and blockers to premium Ax hands. With ~100bb effective I want to take the initiative and build the pot while having fold equity, so I’ll 3-bet to a comfortable size to narrow Elon’s range and get value from worse Aces, broadways and pairs. Raising to 1,000 total is a standard, constructive sizing here — if Elon 4-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (fold to an oversized 4-bet, call or shove depending on sizing); if he calls I’ll use my positional advantage postflop to c-bet and extract value.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 after my SB raise to 300 with Jd8d suited connectors. Pot is 1300, to call 700 (total to 1000), offering ~2.85:1 odds requiring ~35% equity. Jd8d has good playability, straight/flush potential, and sufficient equity (~35-40%) against a typical BB 3-bet range (TT+, AQ+, broadways, suited aces/connectors) in 3-handed deepstack play. Minni's history shows aggression but also folds to pressure; calling keeps pot small OOP, realizes equity well post-flop, and avoids folding a premium speculative hand too tight. Raising risks overcommitting OOP as a bluff; folding is too weak given pot odds and hand strength. Prefer call to see flop in position dynamics.

Flop is K♦7♠5♥, rainbow board with a king high card and disconnected low cards. My J♦8♦ completely misses: no pair, no straight draw (only backdoor), backdoor diamond flush draw at best (3 diamonds total). Equity is low (~20-30%) against Minni's BB 3-bet range (KK+, AK, AQs+, KQs, some suited broadways/Ax, occasional bluffs). OOP after calling her large 3-bet preflop (pot 2000), betting as a bluff risks raising/folding OOP to her likely value-heavy range, with poor playability on turn/river. Check exploits her range (she c-bets often but checks weaker hands), induces bluffs/mistakes, gets free cards if checked behind, and easy fold to her bet. No showdown value to justify check-call. Consistent with history: Minni aggressive but folds to pressure; preserve stack for better spots in 3-handed deepstack play.

I 3-bet preflop to 1,000 with A♠J♠, Elon called. Flop 5h 7s Kd is relatively dry but the K hits a lot of his calling range (Kx, some broadways, medium pairs). I currently have Ace-high plus a backdoor spade draw and I block many A‑combos, so I have decent showdown value and some fold equity as the preflop aggressor. As the 3‑bettor I should c-bet a meaningful proportion of missed hands to pick up the pot and to charge equity draws. A small continuation bet (~1/3–40% pot) keeps pressure, folds many inferior broadways and pocket underpairs, and keeps the pot manageable if he calls. If he calls I can re-evaluate turn play based on the card; if he raises large I can fold or respond based on sizing. Betting ~700 chips is the right balance here.

Facing a 700 bet into a 2000 pot (now 2700, ~3.85:1 odds requiring ~21% equity) on K♦7♠5♥ with J♦8♦, which completely whiffs: jack-high no pair, no straight draw (gutshot needs 9/T but disconnected), only backdoor diamond flush draw (3 diamonds total: my two + K♦). Equity vs Minni's BB 3-bet + c-bet range (KK+, AK, AQs+, KQs, broadway Ax, some suited connectors/bluffs) is ~15-25%, often dominated even when ahead on overs. OOP post her large preflop 3-bet, check-raising as bluff is high-variance/risky (she can jam 8700 stack or call/raise value); check-call commits poorly to turn OOP with no equity; folding exploits her value-heavy range, preserves 9850 stack for better spots in deep 3-handed play. Minni's history: aggressive (c-bets often) but folds to pressure (e.g., Hand 13, 20); check-fold is disciplined GTO/SPM line here, avoiding spew.

3-handed on the button with Td5d, suited connectors with flush potential and straight potential. Excellent position to raise wide pre-flop for value or steal blinds. Standard sizing is 3x BB to 300. Opponents have shown willingness to call small raises in history, but button advantage allows wide range. Deep stacks support post-flop playability.

I have TT in the small blind facing a button open to 300 in a 3-handed pot. Versus a button opening range here, tens are a clear value 3‑bet — they fare well against a wide opening range and I want to both isolate Elon and deny cheap equity to the big blind. Calling 250 keeps me out of position postflop and concedes initiative; a small min‑raise is too weak. I’ll 3‑bet to a standard size of 1,000 total (putting 950 more in), big enough to get value and fold equity but small relative to our deep stacks so I can continue postflop if called. Plan: proceed with pot control and continuation betting on favorable boards; re-evaluate if Elon 4‑bets big.

I am holding 9-3 offsuit in the big blind. Elon raised from the button, and Minni 3-bet from the small blind. This hand is incredibly weak and does not play well post-flop, especially in a 3-bet pot where I would be out of position against both players. There is no strategic reason to call or raise with such a marginal hand.

Facing a 3-bet from Minni in the SB after my BTN open with Td5d. Suited connectors have excellent playability heads-up in position on deep stacks: straight and flush potential, can flop strong hands. Pot odds are excellent at 700 to call into 1400 (3:1), effective pot ~2100 post-call. Minni's SB 3-bet range vs BTN open 3-handed includes value (QQ+, AK) but also bluffs and suited broadways for position OOP playability. Folding is too tight for a speculative hand with position; 4-betting risks bloating pot OOP for her with a hand that doesn't block strong hands well. Calling realizes equity, controls pot size, and leverages position to outplay post-flop. History shows Minni c-bets flops but folds to resistance (e.g., Hand 27).

I flopped a set of tens in a 3‑bet pot — a very strong, mostly value‑betting situation. Elon's calling range to my 3‑bet from the button contains a lot of Kx, broadways, AX and medium pairs that will happily pay to see a flop; there are essentially no real draws to charge on Ts Kd 2c. I want to build the pot and extract value without scaring off one‑pair hands, so I’ll bet around two‑thirds of the pot. That sizing (1400 into ~2100) keeps Kx/Ax and worse pairs and single tens in the calling range while still growing the pot and protecting against the rare runner‑runner weirdness. If Elon raises large I’ll re‑evaluate (he could have a very unlikely 22/KK or a huge bluff) but plan to call reasonably sized raises with trips and continue extracting on later streets.

Middle pair with tens (Td pairs Ts) on a Kd Ts 2c board, plus backdoor diamond flush draw. In position heads-up after calling SB 3-bet pre-flop with suited connectors, which have shown good playability. Minni's flop bet of 1400 into 3500 pot (~40% pot) from her SB 3-bet range (QQ+, AK, suited broadways, some bluffs). Board favors her value range (Kx, sets) but also hits bluffs/draws. Pot odds offer ~2.5:1 (1400 to win 4900), requiring ~28% equity, which middle pair + position + blockers (blocks Tx, Ad for some draws) provides vs polarized range. Calling realizes equity, keeps pot controlled, and leverages position to exploit her OOP on turn (history shows she checks/folds to aggression sometimes, e.g., Hand 13, 20). Raising risks vs Kx (weak kicker) and commits too much (~28% stack) with non-nutted hand; folding too tight for speculative hand that flops pair. Plan: call, evaluate turn dynamics.

Turn Kc drastically changes the math. I flopped a set and built the pot, but the turn pairs the K on the board — exactly the card that lives in a large portion of Elon's calling range to my 3‑bet (he opened then called). Any single King in his hand now has trips and therefore beats my set of tens. Betting or raising here risks bloating the pot when a big chunk of his range is now ahead; I don’t want to barrel into a likely superior holding and get called or raised off my equity. Pot control is the right play: check and evaluate his action. If he leads a small/medium bet I can call to keep weaker Ax/bluffs and medium pairs in the pot and re‑evaluate the river; if he overbets or shoves, I will fold because I’m behind to the Kx combos that called my flop. Action: check to keep the pot manageable and get to river with discretion (pot 4,900; stacks ~Minni 9,000 / Elon 7,450).

Heads-up on the turn with middle pair (Ts) and backdoor diamond flush draw on a Ts Kd 2c Kc board (paired kings). Pot 4900, IP after Minni's check OOP from her SB 3-bet and flop c-bet range (QQ+, AK, suited broadways, some bluffs). Her flop bet suggests Kx, sets, or draws; turn check likely pot control with top pair (AK, KQ), medium pairs (QQ/JJ), or missed draws, less likely monsters (KK+/sets) which might bet for value. My Td5d blocks some Tx and Ad flush draws, has showdown value vs QQ/JJ/A-high, and position to realize equity. Betting half-pot (2500) extracts thin value from worse pairs/A-high, protects vs club flush draws or runner-runner straights (e.g., QJ), applies pressure to induce folds from AK/KQ wary of paired board, and denies equity. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or cheap showdowns; her history shows checking weakness OOP (e.g., Hand 11, 17). Deep stacks (~74bb effective) support controlled aggression; if raised/shoved, can fold middle pair profitably given SPR. Plan: bet 2500, evaluate response.

I flopped a set of tens and intentionally checked the turn to control the pot once the K paired (many of Elon’s calling combos to my 3‑bet contain Ks and now beat me). Elon leads 2,500 into the ~4,900 pot (pot now 7,400) — a medium-sized bet. Folding a set here is too nitty given he still has lots of non‑K hands (AQ/AJ, medium pairs, some bluffs) that will pay to see a river, and the bet gives me ~25% break‑even equity (2,500 / (7,400+2,500) ≈ 25%). Raising is poor: if he has a K I’m crushed and a raise just bloats the pot while rarely folding out better. I’ll call the 2,500 to keep weaker hands and bluffs in the pot and re‑evaluate the river — fold to a large shove or overbet on the river, value/call small river bets or extract when he shows weakness.

I have a vulnerable but strong holding — a set of tens (TcTh) on Ts Kd 2c Kc Jc. Elon's line (open, call of my 3‑bet, call of my flop cbet, then a turn raise when the K paired) is heavily weighted toward Kx combos that now beat me, but it still contains worse hands (AQ/AJ, some medium pairs) and occasional bluffs. Folding a set to a single medium river bet is overly nitty; raising is a mistake because if he holds a King I'm crushed and a raise will mainly fold out worse hands and get called by the hands that already beat me. The river price (~2,500 into the pot) gives me roughly ~25% break‑even equity, so a call is correct to keep worse hands and bluffs in. I also block some two‑club flush combos by holding the Tc, slightly reducing the chance he has a two‑club flush. I will call the 2,500 to extract value from worse and to bluff‑catch, but will respect any very large shove.

River board Ts Kd 2c Kc Jc pairs kings, adds three clubs completing flushes and possible runner-runner straights (e.g., Qx for T-J-Q-K). Holding Td5d for middle pair tens, showdown value vs bluffs/A-high/medium pairs, but crushed by any Kx (AK/KQ/KJ for kings/trips), flushes, or straights. Minni's SB 3-bet pre, flop c-bet, turn check-call 2500 suggests Kx (AK/KQ), sets, or stubborn draws/pairs wary of paired board OOP. Her river check indicates pot control or weakness, unlikely nuts (quads/boat/flush would bet for value). History shows Minni checks weakness OOP (e.g., Hands 11,17,20). Position allows free showdown; betting risks calls from better kings/flushes while thin value from worse unlikely given sizing (pot 9900, stack 4950). Bluffing inefficient vs calling station tendencies post-turn call. Checking maximizes EV by realizing equity without risk.

Button with A♠5♦ vs only the blinds — this is a standard open. I want to seize the initiative, use position and fold equity, and block many A‑x combos, so raise rather than limp. A 3xBB sizing (300 total) is conventional here: big enough to apply pressure to the SB and isolate the short‑stacked BB (Elon, 4.85k) while keeping the raise small relative to my stack (16.4k). Note Elon may shove his short stack; A5o has marginal equity vs a jam range, so I’ll avoid getting committed to big spots unless he oversteps.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Four offsuit. Minni opened from the Button. While we are playing three-handed, King-Four offsuit is still quite weak to defend from the Small Blind out of position. Furthermore, Elon in the Big Blind has a relatively short stack and might shove over a call. Folding is the most prudent play to maintain my stack lead.

T2o is a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or playability post-flop. Facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive in prior hands, the correct play is to fold the big blind. Calling 200 into a 450 pot gives insufficient odds and leaves me out of position with a hand that rarely improves. Raising has no fold equity with this holding. Preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with 2h4c, which is a very weak hand. The pot is small and I only need to call 100 more, but this hand has almost no playability or potential. Looking at the previous hands, Elon and Minni are active, and playing trash cards out of position or in multi-way pots will likely lead to unnecessary chip loss. I will fold.

Preflop in SB, button folded, facing BB with Qd6s offsuit. This is a weak hand with poor playability out of position. Queen-high has some value but 6 kicker is bad, no straight/flush potential. Folding the SB is standard with trash to avoid difficult postflop decisions OOP, especially against a potentially wide BB defense. Preserving stack for better spots over 150 hands.

3-handed on the button with SB (Minni) folded (dead money in pot), BB (Flash) checked, facing an unopened pot of 150. K2o is a profitable open-raise from the button vs BB due to excellent position, high fold equity against a checking BB range, and the king blocker reducing BB's top pair/top kicker combos. BB folds frequently to button opens based on history (e.g., Hands 7, 13, 21, 28 where opponents folded pre-flop to my opens). Standard sizing is 3x BB to 300, building pot while maintaining fold equity. My 48BB stack can handle post-flop play if needed, but primarily stealing blinds. Limping is suboptimal as it kills fold equity and plays out of position OOP post-flop.

I have 3c9s — a very weak, offsuit, non-connected hand with poor playability. Elon opened to 300 from the button and Flash (BB) still has to act, so calling 250 more puts me out of position against at least one (likely two) players with ranges that dominate my hand. A raise would be a thin bluff with little fold equity here and risks a 3-bet from Flash or Elon. With a healthy 16,550 stack and many better spots to invest in, the +EV play is to conserve chips and fold this junk from the small blind.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Queen offsuit, a premium starting hand. Elon has raised from the Button to 300. Given Elon's relatively short stack (45 BBs) and his frequent opening range from the button, I want to 3-bet for value and to take control of the pot. A raise to 1000 is a standard sizing that puts pressure on his opening range and allows me to comfortably play for his remaining stack if he decides to push or call. If he shoves, I will likely call given the strength of my hand relative to a button opening/shoving range.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 (3.33x) from Flash in the BB after my standard button open to 300 with K2o. In position, but K2o is a marginal open-raise hand with poor post-flop playability, low equity (~30-35%) vs. a polarized BB 3-bet range (QQ+, AK, some suited broadways/bluffs). Calling 700 into 1350 pot offers ~3:1 odds but requires hitting king or running good, while OOP opponent (though I'm IP) can put pressure. 4-bet bluff possible with K blocker reducing villain's KK/AK value, min-raise to 1700 leaves 2800 behind (flexible), but Flash has shown strength in big pots (Hand 15 4-bet shove vs. AA) and may continue. History shows opponents fold pre often, but folding weak hands here preserves my 45BB stack for premium spots in 3-handed dynamic. Pot odds not compelling for call, fold equity on 4-bet uncertain vs. aggressive BB. Standard fold to avoid difficult post-flop decisions with trash.

I'm on the button with Jc2c — a marginal hand but suited and with great position. Against passive blinds and given my typical opening tendencies at this table, I want to seize initiative and attempt a button steal. A standard 3BB open (to 300 total) is the size I use here: it pressures the deep SB (Flash) and forces Elon (short stack ~44BB) to make a decision. If Elon shoves I will fold most of the time because J2s is easily dominated and the pot odds won't justify a call; if called I'll play postflop aggressively in position, leveraging backdoor club equity and fold equity.

I am in the Small Blind with 5s3s. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While 5s3s is a suited connector that can play well post-flop, playing out of position against an aggressive opponent like Minni is disadvantageous. Elon (the Big Blind) is very short-stacked with only 4400, meaning a 3-bet or a call from me might induce a shove from him, which I would have to fold to. Given the stack depths and my position, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my healthy stack of 18900.

4c6d is a weak offsuit connector with poor playability and low equity against Minni's button raising range, which includes many better hands. Pot odds (200 to call into 450) require about 31% equity, but this hand rarely makes strong hands and has bad implied odds. Folding preserves my short stack (4400) for better spots. No history suggests Minni folds enough to 3-bet bluff profitably here.

I am on the button with J2o. This is a very weak hand. While the button offers a positional advantage, J2o doesn't play well post-flop and the blinds (Elon and Minni) have been relatively active and aggressive. Elon has a short stack of 43.5 big blinds, which makes him more likely to commit or shove over an open. Minni has been 3-betting frequently in previous hands. Folding is the most prudent play here to wait for better opportunities.

K3o is a weak offsuit King-high hand, ranking low in preflop hand strength (around top 65%). From the SB, facing a competent BB like Minni who has defended aggressively in past hands (e.g., calling raises with draws or marginal hands), completing the 50 into 200 pot (3:1 odds) seems tempting due to dead money, but postflop play OOP with poor playability, dominated kickers on Kx boards, and no strong draws makes it -EV. Raising is suboptimal as K3o blocks few strong hands and folds out worse too often, risking tough OOP spots if called/3bet. Folding preserves stack for better positions/hands; history shows profitable discipline folding trash preflop (e.g., hands 5,10,23,30,32). Stack 43BB allows patient play over 150 hands.

Folded to me on the button after SB likely folded and BB checked, creating a prime blind-stealing opportunity. K4o is a playable hand here with a strong king blocker, good high-card value, and my position allows controlling the pot postflop. Opponents have deep stacks, but my 43BB stack supports a standard raise size. Raising to 300 (3x BB) applies pressure, builds the pot with initiative, and has good fold equity against likely weak blind ranges. Folding is too tight; calling/limping is suboptimal without position advantage exploitation.

I'm in the small blind with 4s5d facing a button open to 300. This is a marginal but defendable hand vs a BTN 3x open — it has decent equity and can flop strong draws/two-pairs, and the call is cheap. Pot odds: calling 250 into a 450 pot gives a break-even equity around ~26%, which 4-5o generally meets vs a BTN opening range. I don't want to 3-bet with this hand (Elon is only ~40BB and could 3-bet/shove, or we could get isolated OOP postflop). Plan: call to see the flop, play cautiously out of position — continue only with strong pair/two-pair/straight draws or good turn improvements, otherwise fold to heavy aggression.

With Ace-Four offsuit in the Big Blind, I am facing a standard raise from the Button and a call from the Small Blind. I am getting very good pot odds (3.5:1) to close the action and see a flop. Elon, the Button, has a relatively short stack (around 40 BBs), which makes 3-betting as a squeeze less ideal because he may commit his remaining chips with a wide range, forcing me to either fold away my equity or call with a marginal hand. Calling allows me to play post-flop while keeping the pot manageable.

I have 4♠5♦ on a 3♣-T♣-6♠ flop. I have an open-ended straight draw (need a 2 or 7) — ~8 outs (~31.5% to hit by the river, ~17% to hit on the turn). This is a multiway pot and I'm out of position against both Elon (BTN) and Flash (BB). Against multiple opponents and with no pair, I prefer pot control and to realize my equity rather than firing a semi-bluff OOP into likely made hands (tens, pairs, club draws). Plan: check and see a free turn. If faced with a small continuation bet (roughly ≤ ~2/3 pot) I'll call because the pot odds + implied odds justify it; fold to large bets/raises unless I improve. If checked to on the turn and I pick up a pair or improve my draw, I'll consider betting/raising for value or fold equity.

On a flop of 3c-Tc-6s, I have an overcard (Ace) and a backdoor nut flush draw with the Ace of clubs. Since Minni checked and I am in the Big Blind acting next, the most standard play is to check to the pre-flop aggressor (Elon) who is on the button. Leading out (donking) into two opponents on this board texture with just an overcard and a backdoor draw is not ideal. I will check and see how the button reacts.

Preflop I raised K4o from the button as a standard blind steal, which got two callers from the blinds, building a 900 pot. Flop is 3c-Tc-6s, a somewhat coordinated board with two clubs and spade, possible straight draws (45, 57) and flush draws, but rainbow-ish otherwise. Both blinds checked to me, representing weakness or traps, but given their passive history in many spots (e.g., frequent checks postflop), they likely hold marginal hands without top pair or better. My button raise range is wide, including overpairs, sets, two-pair, top pair, and bluffs/draws, so I have strong equity realization and fold equity here. King-high has some showdown value and blocks Kx, with backdoor spade potential. Position allows control: c-bet to deny equity to draws/weak pairs, take it down ~50-60% of time. Half-pot bet of 450-600 protects against floats and builds pot for value if strong. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws/improvers and forfeits initiative. With 40BB stack, committed little so far (~7%), betting 600 (2/3 pot) is standard sizing balancing value/bluff. Plan: if raised, fold; if called, evaluate turn with position.

I have 4♠5♦ on 3♣-T♣-6♠ — an open-ended straight draw (2s or 7s = 8 outs). Equity ≈31.5% to the river (≈17% to the turn). Elon (BTN) c-bet 600 into a 1500 pot (~0.4 pot) which gives me ~28.6% pot odds to call (600/(1500+600)). That’s marginally +EV versus my draw, and in a multiway, out-of-position spot I prefer to realize equity rather than pot-control-busting semi-bluff/raise. Raising here would risk bloating the pot OOP and invite isolation or a shove from Elon (shorter stack) or a call/3-bet from the BB. Plan: call the 600 to see the turn. If I hit the straight (or pair up) I can play aggressively; if I don’t improve and face large aggression I’ll fold to pressure.

On a board of 3c-Tc-6s, I am holding Ace-high (Ac 4d) with the backdoor nut flush draw. Elon (the pre-flop aggressor) has bet 600 into a 900 pot, and Minni has already called that bet in the Small Blind. This multi-way action significantly reduces the value of my Ace-high. Even if I pair my Ace on the turn, my kicker is weak and I could easily be out-kicked by hands like AT, AJ, or AQ. Additionally, Elon is relatively short-stacked (less than 30 BBs remaining), which limits the implied odds for my backdoor flush draw. Calling 600 into a growing pot when I am likely in third place and out of position is a losing play. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I just turned a made straight. With 4♠5♦ on 3♣-T♣-6♠-8♠ I have 3-4-5-6 — currently the best hand vs. Elon’s range (he c-bet the flop then checked turn, so he’s likely on one-pair/top-pair or a controlling hand). This is a clear value-bet spot heads-up: I want to charge single-card draws and extract calls from tens, pocket pairs and weaker holdings rather than slow-play and allow a free river. I’ll size to keep weaker pairs and draws calling — bet ~½–⅔ pot. Pot is 2,100 so I’ll bet 1,200. If he flats we proceed to river for more value; if he jams I’ll be happy to call — my straight is effectively best now and only rarely outdrawn on the river.

Preflop button raise with K4o got calls from both blinds, leading to a bloated pot. Flop 3c-Tc-6s was drawish but I c-bet for value/protection/fold equity with king-high and position, getting called by Minni only. Turn 8s adds another spade, completing spade flush draws (6s-8s) and improving straight draws (e.g., 45 now has straight + backdoor flush, 57 straight). Board now highly coordinated. Minni's check-call flop then turn lead of 1200 into 2100 suggests strength: likely top pair (T), turned pair (8), flush draw that improved, or two-pair/set. Her range excludes total air given aggression. My K4o is pure king-high bluff-catcher with zero equity realization—no pair, no draw, wrong suits. Pot odds ~3.75:1 require ~21% equity, but vs her polarized leading range (value + semi-bluffs), king-high has poor equity (<15%, loses to pairs/draws dominating). Raising is a bluff into likely strong hands, no fold equity on her initiative. Calling commits 35% of stack OOP with trash, bad SPR. Folding preserves 3450 stack (43BB) for better spots in 150-hand game. Opponents passive historically but this line is strong; live to fight another hand.

5♦6♠ on the button is a marginal but playable hand — good to open in position to seize initiative and steal the blinds. Pot is 150 and calling 100 is passive; a standard open to 300 is appropriate: large enough to fold out weak blind defense and to give me postflop initiative, but small enough that if Elon (BB, ~33.5BB) 3-bets or shoves I can fold without committing too much with a weak holding. If called, I will use position to c-bet favorable flops and realize equity. Raise to 300.

I have been dealt pocket kings (Kh Ks) in the Small Blind. This is the second-best starting hand in Texas Hold'em. Minni (the Button) has opened for 300. Elon (the Big Blind) is extremely short-stacked with only 3350 total. My goal is to extract maximum value and potentially isolate Minni or get Elon to commit his stack. A standard 3-bet from the Small Blind against a 300 open should be around 3x to 4x the open size, particularly with a short stack behind. I will raise to 1000. This is large enough to build a pot but keeps Elon's stack in play for a potential shove, while making it expensive for Minni to continue with speculative hands.

Preflop in BB with Ac3s facing a button raise to 300 and SB 3-bet to 1000 (900 to call into 1400 pot). Pot odds require ~39% equity, but A3s is dominated by typical 3-bet ranges (e.g., pairs, better aces, broadways) and plays poorly OOP postflop, especially multiway if Minni calls. Suitedness helps slightly for speculative value, but stack depth (33BB) and position make folding correct to preserve stack for better spots. No strong blockers or playability justifies a call or 3-bet.

I opened to 300 on the button with 5♦6♠ and Flash (SB) 3‑bet to 1,000. Effective stacks are deep (~176bb), so implied odds matter, and I have position. Calling costs 700 more into a 1,400 pot (final pot ~2,100) — break‑even equity ≈33%. 5‑6o has decent equity vs a reasonably wide SB 3‑bet range and plays well in position; 4‑betting as a bluff with 56o is too thin and folding surrenders good implied odds for a small price. Plan: call to see a flop in position, c‑bet or apply pressure only when I connect or get favorable texture, otherwise control the pot and fold to heavy action. I will call 700 (1000 total committed).

I have pocket Kings (Kh Ks) on a flop of Ad 7d 9h. This is a very coordinated board with an Ace, which is the worst possible card for me. In a 3-bet pot (Pre-flop: Flash 1000, Minni call), the range of a player who calls a 3-bet from the Button often includes hands like AQ, AJ, or sometimes even slow-played AK, though Minni might have 4-bet AK. My hand is now a bluff catcher. However, as the pre-flop aggressor, I still have a range advantage that includes AA, AK, and sets like 77/99. I will check to see how Minni reacts. If she bets small, I may call one street, but if she bets large, I must be cautious as she could easily have an Ace. Checking also allows me to control the pot size and potentially induce bluffs from missed draws (like hearts or straight draws).

Flash was the preflop 3‑bettor and checked the Ace‑9‑7 (A♥9♥7♦) flop. With 5♦6♠ I have no pair, no flush draw and only a very weak straight draw (primarily an 8 completes). This board heavily favors the 3‑bettor’s range (many Ax and strong broadways), so a c‑bet would be a thin bluff against hands that will call. With deep stacks and position I prefer pot control and to realize equity rather than firing a marginal bluff. Plan: check and see the turn for free; call small turn bets to continue with the draw, fold to large aggression, and re‑evaluate if the turn improves my hand or the board becomes more favorable for a semi‑bluff.

The turn brings a second 9, pairing the board. My Kings are still behind any Ace or any 9. However, Minni's check on the flop (Ah 7d 9h) is a significant piece of information; players often bet an Ace on such a coordinated board to protect against draws. Her check suggests she might have a medium pocket pair (like 88, TT, JJ, or QQ) or a draw that she wanted a free card for. By checking again, I am practicing pot control and giving her the opportunity to bluff with missed draws or continue checking with marginal made hands. I am not yet ready to fold to a single bet given her flop passivity, but I want to keep the pot size manageable.

Check. I hold 5♦6♠ on A♥9♥7♦9♦ — no pair, no heart/flush draw and only 4 clean outs (any 8) to make a straight (~8.7% on the river). Flash was the preflop 3‑bettor and has checked flop and turn; that line is often weakness (or a slow‑play), but either way I don't want to bloat a pot with essentially zero equity. Pot is 2,100 and effective stacks are deep (~17k), so a bluff here risks too much and a value bet is impossible. I’ll check to keep the pot small, let him reveal strength on the river, fold to meaningful aggression, and only continue if I actually hit the 8 (then convert to value) or he makes a very small river stab I can call profitably.

The board is 7d-Ah-9h-9d-3h. I have pocket Kings, which were an overpair pre-flop but are now beat by any Ace or any 9. However, Minni's behavior is telling: she called my 3-bet pre-flop but checked back on both the flop and the turn. On a coordinated board with an Ace and a flush draw, an opponent with an Ace or a set will almost always bet for value and protection. Her checks strongly suggest she has a marginal made hand like JJ, QQ, or perhaps a medium-sized pocket pair that she is trying to take to showdown. The river 3h completes the heart flush draw. While I could bet for thin value against hands like JJ or QQ, checking is the safer play to realize my showdown value. If she bets after checking twice, she is either bluffing with a missed straight draw or she backed into a flush. I will check and likely call a reasonable bet.

I have no pair, no heart/flush draw, and no straight — essentially zero showdown value on A♥9♥7♦9♦3♥. Flash was the preflop 3‑bettor but then checked flop, turn and now the river; that line can be both weakness or a slow‑play, and I can’t credibly extract value with 5♦6♠. Betting here would be a pure bluff into a player who showed preflop strength and could have an Ace, a nine, or a slow‑played strong hand; with deep stacks a failed bluff risks too much for little reward. Checking preserves my stack, lets Flash either show weakness or bluff first, and keeps me able to fold to meaningful aggression. I’ll check and fold to a sizable river bet, only calling a very small stab if it’s priced attractively.

I am on the button with 7-2 offsuit, which is widely considered the worst starting hand in Texas Hold'em. There is no strategic advantage to playing this hand, even from the button. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

T5o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity, dominated potential, and low playability. In SB facing no preflop action after BTN fold, standard is to defend wide vs BB, but this is among the bottom of ranges. Folding avoids difficult OOP postflop spots with trash equity vs BB calling range. Stack 33BB, no need to gamble. Realize fold equity by checking behind or whatever, but preserve for better hands.

3-handed on the button with K8o (33BB stack). This is a standard opening hand in a loose short-handed game. K8o has good playability postflop, a strong king blocker, and high card strength. Opponents have shown passive tendencies in history (frequent checks, calls, folds to aggression). Raising builds the pot in position or steals blinds. Standard size 3x BB.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and I (SB) hold A♠7♥. This is a marginal but defendable hand — the ace blocker helps and the raise is only 3bb, but I'm out of position and Flash (BB) is deep and can easily squeeze. I don't want to 3-bet and either isolate Elon (who can jam ~30bb) or induce a big squeeze from Flash with a mediocre ace. Calling keeps the pot manageable, gives good pot odds to see a flop, and lets me play aggressively on ace-high boards or pot-control otherwise. I'll call and re-evaluate on the flop.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Queen offsuit, a very strong premium hand. The Button (Elon) has raised to 3BB, and the Small Blind (Minni) has called. I have two main options: call to see a flop with a disguised hand or 3-bet for value and to squeeze the pot. Given Elon's short stack (only 3,000 total), a raise here is very effective as it puts significant pressure on him and builds a pot against Minni's calling range. I will raise to 1,200 to take control of the hand and potentially end it pre-flop or isolate.

Preflop on the button with K8o (30BB effective). Opened standardly to 300, SB flatted, BB 3-bets to 1200 (4x, large size). Facing a strong BB 3-bet range after a flat (likely QQ+, AKo/s, some KQs, maybe bluffs but size suggests value). K8o offsuit is king-high with poor playability, dominated often by calling/3-betting hands. Calling commits 900 (30% stack) into 1800 pot (pot odds ~33%, but equity <30% vs range, low SPR ~0.7 postflop leaves 1800 behind into 2700 pot). 4-bet shoving 2700 more has poor equity vs calling range and low fold equity with deep BB/SB OOP who get good odds. History shows opponents passive but Flash 3-bets aggressively when strong (e.g., Hand 15). Best to fold marginal open to resistance and preserve stack for better spots in 3-handed game.

I stick with my earlier plan to call. I have A♠7♥ — an ace with a useful blocker and some post‑flop playability — and already have 300 in, so calling the extra 900 (total 1,200) is the right, low‑risk defense. The pot is ~1,800 so a 900 call gives ~2,700 to win, roughly 3:1 pot odds (≈25% equity needed). I’m out of position, so I don’t want to 4‑bet and bloat the pot with a marginal hand; a flat keeps the pot manageable and lets me realize equity on A‑high or favorable flop textures. Plan: see the flop and continue aggressively on ace‑high or coordinated boards where my blockers help; otherwise control the pot and fold to heavy action if I miss.

I have A♠7♥ on a 3♠ J♥ 5♠ flop. I only have Ace-high (no pair) with a backdoor, runner-runner spade possibility and the useful Ace blocker, and I’m out of position vs Flash who 3-bet preflop (likely a value‑heavy/polarized range). Betting OOP into a 3‑bettor with just Ace‑high bloats a pot I don’t want to play big — I’d prefer to keep options and pot‑control. Plan: check and let Flash act. If he checks back I get a free turn to improve; if he c‑bets a small size (~≤1/3 pot) I’ll call to realize equity and use my Ace blocker; if he fires a large bet (>~1/2 pot) I’ll fold most of the time because I’m usually behind and don’t have the odds. I won’t try a bluff‑raise OOP; I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn and only invest more if I pair my Ace, pick up a clear draw, or get a very favorable action pattern from him.

I am the pre-flop aggressor with Ace-Queen offsuit after my squeeze to 1,200. The flop is Jack-high with a flush draw. Although I missed the board, I have two overcards and the range advantage as the 3-better. Minni, who called pre-flop, has checked to me. A continuation bet is standard here to maintain pressure and potentially take the pot down immediately. A small bet size of about 1/3 pot (900) is effective on this board texture to keep her range honest while not overcommitting if she has a Jack or a draw.

I have A♠7♥ on a 3♠ J♥ 5♠ flop. I only have Ace‑high (no pair) and only a runner‑runner spade possibility, but my A♠ is a useful blocker to a lot of strong A‑x and spade combos. Flash 3‑betted pre and now fires a very small c‑bet (900 into a 3600 pot ≈ 25% pot). That bet gives me very favorable pot odds — I only need ~20% equity (900/(3600+900)) to make a call. Given the small sizing (likely includes a fair number of bluffs) and my blocker + showdown value vs some of his bluffs, I’ll call the 900 to see the turn and re‑evaluate. Plan: fold to heavy turn aggression if I don’t improve; call small‑to‑moderate turn bets to realize equity; if I pair my Ace or pick up clear equity I can become aggressive on later streets.

I have A♠7♥ on 3♠ J♥ 5♠ 2♦ and I’m first to act on the turn with pot ≈4,500 and nothing to call. I missed — only Ace‑high with a runner‑runner spade possibility — and Flash’s line (3‑bet preflop, small flop c‑bet) contains a mix of bluffs, floats and value hands. Betting OOP here with no pair would bloat the pot and put me in awkward spots against hands that beat me; a bluff is thin and a raise from him would be very painful. Checking keeps the pot under control, lets me realize showdown value versus his weaker holdings and bluffs, and preserves the option to call a small river bet or fold to heavy aggression. Plan: check the turn; if he barrels the river I’ll call small/medium bets as a bluff‑catch when appropriate given my blocker, fold to large river aggression, and if I pair my Ace or pick up clear equity I’ll become aggressive.

On the turn, the 2nd of diamonds is largely a blank card that doesn't significantly change the board texture. Having been called on the flop, Minni likely has a Jack, a pocket pair like 88-TT, or a spade flush draw. My Ace-high still has some showdown value against her draws, but it is unlikely to fold out a Jack with a single bet here. By checking back, I control the pot size and take a free card to potentially hit an Ace or Queen on the river, or simply reach showdown cheaply if she is also drawing.

Check. I only have A♠7♥ (ace‑high) on a 3♠ J♥ 5♠ 2♦ 3♥ board — I never improved. Flash’s line (3‑bet pre → tiny ~25% pot c‑bet on the flop → turn check) is consistent with a mix of marginal value (Jx, 5x, medium pockets) and floats/missed draws. Betting OOP here with no pair is a thin bluff: small bets will often be called by hands that beat me and large bets risk a raise that I can’t comfortably handle out of position. My A‑blocker helps a bit against A‑x value, but not enough to make a profitable river bluff. I’ll control the pot and check to showdown. If Flash barrels a small river bet (≈≤1/3 pot) I’ll treat it as a bluff‑catch and call occasionally; I’ll fold to larger sizing or raise‑shoves.

On the river, the board is 3s-Jh-5s-2d-3h. I have Ace-high (Ad Qs). Pre-flop, I squeezed to 1,200 and Minni called. On the flop, I bet 900 and she called. The turn went check-check. Now, Minni has checked the river for the third time. Given the action, she likely has a marginal hand like a Jack, a pocket pair (66-TT), or a missed spade draw. My Ace-high has decent showdown value against her missed draws (like KQ of spades or other high card hands). I am unlikely to fold out a Jack or a pocket pair with a single bet here, and checking allows me to realize my equity and reach showdown for free. If she has a better hand, she would have likely bet herself or will call any bet I make. Checking is the most prudent play.

Button with AdTs is a clear open-raise spot. Pot is 150, to-call 100; opponents: Flash (SB) very deep and likely to defend wide, Elon (BB) short (~29bb). I want to take the initiative, isolate the short-stacked BB and keep the postflop SPR reasonable. A standard 3x BB open to 300 gives good fold equity, induces shoves/calls from Elon where ATo fares well, and avoids the passive multiway scenarios that limp/call would create. If Flash 3-bets big I will re-evaluate based on sizing; if Elon shoves I will call. Raising to 300 balances aggression and pot control here.

I am in the Small Blind with K2s. Minni opened for 300 from the Button. Elon, in the Big Blind, is extremely short-stacked with only 29 BBs remaining. K2s is a marginal hand that plays poorly out of position, and calling invites a potential squeeze from the short stack in the BB. Given the 3x open size and my position, folding is the most disciplined choice to avoid being trapped in a bloated pot with a weak hand.

Facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds, in BB with 29BB stack. J8s is a strong suited connector with good playability, straight and flush potential. Pot odds are favorable (calling 200 more into 450 pot for ~3:1). Button ranges are wide, so equity is decent heads-up. Defend wide from BB vs late position open. Calling keeps pot small, allows realizing equity post-flop, and maintains fold equity on draws. Folding too tight; 3-betting risks too much stack with speculative hand when calling achieves similar goals cheaper.

Flop Jd Kh As gives me top pair (Js) with a strong spade nut flush draw (8 outs to nuts), plus backdoor straight potential. Board is dry-ish but coordinates with button's wide opening range (Ax, Kx, broadway, suited connectors). As BB defender with J8s, this crushes many of his floats and medium strength hands. First to act, pot 650, my remaining stack ~2400 (24BB). Betting extracts value from worse pairs/draws (KQ, QTs, lower pairs, gutshots), protects against his equity (e.g., spade draws, AT), and denies equity. Check allows free cards or cheap river calls we don't want. Size ~2/3 pot (433, rounded to 450) balances value/protection without overcommitting short stack. Vs Minni's aggressive history, betting maintains initiative.

Flop: Jd Kh As. I hold AdTs — top pair with a decent kicker. Elon is short (~22.5bb) and made a small 450 lead into a 1,100 pot (~40% pot), which looks like either a probing c-bet or a thin value bet from a capped/wide calling range. Raising to the minimum (900 total) does several things: it extracts value from worse A-x, Kx, Jx and medium pocket pairs, denies equity to many turn/river draw cards, and maximizes fold equity against his weaker hands. I’m happy to face a shove here — if he jams he’s polarized toward very strong two-pair/sets or bluffs, and calling is correct enough with top pair/top kicker given his preflop calling tendencies. If he simply calls, I keep initiative and can continue for value or control on the turn. Action: raise to 900.

Facing a flop raise from Minni on a strong Jd Kh As board with top pair Js (weak 8 kicker) and backdoor spade flush/straight potential. Already invested 450 on the flop donk, pot 2000, calling additional 450 offers great implied odds (~5.4:1) but leaves SPR ~0.7 post-call (my remaining 1800 into 2450 pot), making us committed anyway with a hand vulnerable to domination (AK, AQ, KQ, AJ better). Folding top pair OOP vs button range too tight. Raising all-in (22BB shove into 2000 pot) maximizes fold equity against her polarized raising range: she folds medium pairs/draws/gutshots (QT, ATs, lower pairs) that we beat or are racing, while value extracting thinner from worse top pairs (AJ weaker kicker, KJ). Monsters (sets, two-pair) snap us off but that's fine post-flop equity denial. Minni's aggressive history (frequent raises but folds to 3bets/shoves like hand 13,20,26) suggests she won't call light with 2250 into ~4250. As short stack in cash game grinding 150 hands, jamming speculative TP in low SPR spot exploits wide button opens and builds stack aggressively without folding equity loss from flat. Superior to calling multiway potential or turn vulnerability.

Board Jd Kh As and I hold AdTs — top pair, top kicker. Preflop Elon called a wide BB range; on the flop he led 450, I 3-bet to 900 and he 4-bet to 2250. Pot is 3800; I must call 1350 more (2250 total). Calling requires ~26% equity (1350 / (3800+1350) ≈ 26%). Against a BB who called preflop and takes this aggressive line his range is polarized (two-pair/sets and some strong single-pair hands, but also plenty of Ax/Kx/Jx and bluffs). AdTs is ahead of many of those combos and has more than the ~26% needed, so a call is correct: it gets the right pot odds, keeps worse A/K hands and bluffs in the pot, and avoids bloating the pot by raising into a range that rarely folds. Plan: call 2250 now, then re-evaluate on the turn — proceed for value on safe cards, fold to extreme shove-lines when the board and action clearly beat me.

Pot is 5150 with only 450 chips left (less than 10% pot), making me pot-committed regardless of action. Board Jd Kh As Qd is extremely coordinated: Broadway straight draws (T9/T10), diamond flush draw, overcards everywhere. Top pair Jacks (Js) with trash 8 kicker is vulnerable to any Ax (AK/AQ/AJ/AT), Kx (KQ/KT), Qx, two-pair combos, sets, or improved draws. However, Minni's flop call of my 2250 3-bet after her raise suggests a polarized range: strong value (AK/AQ/AJ/KQ/sets/Ax) or stubborn draws (QJ/KT/JT/diamond draws/QT). Shoving all-in gets thin value from weaker Ax (A10/A9 with worse kicker), naked Qx/Kx, or bluffs/draws while denying her equity on unimproved draws. Checking OOP invites her bet (likely as aggressor), forcing a fold or worse call-off. Equity realization is best by jamming now: vs value hands ~25% (dominated), vs draws ~50%+, overall decent given history of Minni folding to pressure (e.g., Hands 13,20,26,34). In short-stack cash grind, exploit low SPR by getting it in with showdown value + fold equity vs wide button range. Superior to passive play.

actions.message: There is no reason to complete, bet, or raise since every other player has either folded or gone all-in.

actions.thought: I am on the button with Q8o in a 3-handed game. Q8o is a standard opening hand from the button to put pressure on the blinds, especially with my significant chip lead. I will raise to 300 to attempt to take down the blinds or play the hand in position.

Preflop in SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). My hand is 3s4h, a weak offsuit gapped connector with poor playability out of position. Button ranges are wide but still include many strong hands; calling OOP with this hand has low equity realization and poor implied odds given my 55BB stack and the hand's trash nature. Folding is standard to preserve stack for better spots. No history suggests Flash is raising super wide here to justify a call.

I’m in the big blind with A♥9♠ facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and it costs me 200 more (total 300) to see a flop. A9s is a strong defend vs a button open but I’m out of position and don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal 3-bet; with ~122bb effective calling preserves pot control and keeps my range wide. Plan: call to see the flop; proceed aggressively if I hit top pair or strong draws, otherwise use pot control and fold to heavy action when missed.

I have A♠9♥ (A9s) on K♠ T♦ 9♦ — second pair with the best kicker, out of position. The button checked to me, which often signals missed equity or pot control; this texture (two diamonds + K/T overcards) also runs heavy with hands that beat me (Kx, Tx, sets) and with diamond/straight draws. Betting here would bloat the pot and commit me OOP with a vulnerable pair; a small bet could get raised by stronger made hands and a large bet is unnecessary for protection. Plan: keep pot control and realize equity — check back the flop, call small-to-medium turn bets, and only punish if I improve (A or 9) or get clear read/bluff frequency from the opponent.

On a board of Ks-Td-9d, I have a gutshot straight draw (needing a Jack). The board is quite coordinated and likely hits Minni's calling range from the big blind (hands like JT, QJ, T9, or diamond draws). Since Minni checked, I will put out a continuation bet of 450 to try and take the pot down now, while also setting up a semi-bluff. If I am called, I still have the possibility of hitting a Jack or potentially turning more equity.

I have A♥9♠ on K♠ T♦ 9♦ — second pair with the Ace kicker facing Flash’s 450 c‑bet into a 1100 pot. The sizing is relatively small and represents a wide c‑betting range (Kx/Tx, lots of missed Ax, diamond/straight draws and some pairs). Calling preserves pot control OOP, keeps worse hands and bluffs in, and gets correct pot odds (call 450 to win ~1550 → ~29% required equity). Raising OOP would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and folding concedes too much value. Plan: call, see the turn, fold to heavy turn aggression if I don’t improve; if I pair my A or 9 I’ll extract value/raise accordingly.

I have A♠9♥ (second pair with the best kicker) on K♠ T♦ 9♦ 2♣ and there is no bet to call on the turn. Flash checked to me, which often indicates a missed c-bet hand or pot-control with medium strength (Kx/Tx/weak draws) — betting OOP here would bloat the pot and expose me to raises from better hands. I want to keep the pot manageable, realize my equity, and induce river mistakes/bluffs. Plan: check back the turn, call reasonable river bets from weaker pairs or bluffs, and only get aggressive if I improve to trips or pick up a clear read on a bluff.

On the turn, the board is Ks-Td-9d-2c. I still have a gutshot straight draw (needing a Jack). Minni checked again after calling my flop bet. The Deuce of clubs is a blank. While Minni calling the flop suggests they have some part of this board (likely a Ten, a Nine, or a diamond draw), their check on the turn shows weakness or at least a lack of initiative. I will fire a second barrel of 1000 into the 1550 pot to continue the pressure and try to force a fold from marginal made hands like mid-pairs or draws that might not want to continue. If called, I have outs to a straight on the river.

I call 1000. Preflop and flop I called to keep the pot manageable OOP with A9 and to realize equity. The turn 2♣ is a blank and Flash’s 1,000 into ~1,550 (~65% pot) represents a mix of value (Kx, Tx, sets) and continued bluffs/semi‑bluffs. Calling costs 1,000 to win ~3,550 (pot after call), so I need roughly 28% equity — my second pair with the Ace kicker, plus blockers to some Ax combos and possible bluffs in his range, gives me roughly that equity versus his continuing range. Raising would bloat the pot OOP and be dominated by his value hands; folding would be too nitty because many of his hands are worse or are bluffs. Plan: call now to keep worse hands and bluffs in, re-evaluate on the river — extract value if I improve (9 or A) or fold to very large river polarization/shoves when I miss.

I have A9 (second pair with the best kicker) on K♠ T♦ 9♦ 2♣ J♣. Villain opened BTN, c‑bet on the flop (450) and barreled the turn (1,000) — a line that contains Kx/Tx/9x/sets and also missed diamond/straight bluffs. He checked the river to me, which looks like either missed draws or pot‑control with medium value; that check massively reduces my thin value targets and increases the likelihood that a bet will be called by hands that already beat me (Kx/Tx) or fold out only pure air. Pot is ~3,550 and I have no bet to call; betting OOP here would bloat the pot with a marginal holding and creates little fold equity versus a competent opponent. Checking preserves showdown with a hand that has real value versus bluffs and worse pairs, avoids getting raised off my hand, and keeps the pot comfortable. I will check to get to showdown rather than bluff/value-bet into a capped range.

I have a King-high straight (K-Q-J-T-9) using the Queen in my hand and the K-J-T-9 on the board. The river Jack completed the sequence. Minni has checked to me for the third time. While the Jack is a bit of a 'scare card' that could give her the same straight if she has any Queen, or a higher straight if she has AQ, I must bet for value. She could easily be calling with two-pair hands (like KT, T9, KJ) or sets that are now losing to my straight. I will bet 2400 into the 3550 pot to maximize value from these hands, though I must be wary of a check-raise from AQ.

Board K♠ T♦ 9♦ 2♣ J♣ — I hold A♥9♠ (second pair, best kicker). Flash led flop (450), barreled turn (1000) and now bets the river 2400 into a 5950 pot. Calling 2400 to win ~8350 requires ~29% equity (2400 / (5950+2400) ≈ 28.7%).

Villain’s line is value‑heavy (Kx, Tx, two‑pair combos, sets) but also contains missed diamond/straight semi‑bluffs — he has incentive to barrel a lot of turns as both value and bluffs from the button. My A9 beats many of the bluffs and weaker one‑pair hands and our Ace blocks some strong Ax combos.
