Raising OOP with only second pair is thin: it bloats the pot when behind, loses to the value hands that are continuing, and will fold out only a small portion of his bluffs; folding throws away too much equity.

Given the pot odds, my hand’s showdown value, and villain’s mixed barreling range, the +EV line is to call and see showdown rather than fold or bluff‑raise. I’ll call 2400 to extract value from bluffs/weaker pairs and avoid getting away from a hand that hits the board reasonably often.

AKo is a premium hand, one of the best in poker. On the button with folds to me, this is a standard raising spot to either steal the blinds (150 in pot) or build the pot with a strong range when called. Raising to 3x the big blind (300) is standard sizing here, putting pressure on the blinds who may be wide-ranging. My stack of 5550 allows for deep play postflop if needed. Opponents have shown aggression in past hands but no specific reads yet warrant deviation from GTO.

I’m in the small blind with 9♣4♦ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, 250 to call). 9‑4 offsuit is a very weak, frequently dominated hand and I’ll be out of position post‑flop. Calling 250 to play OOP against Elon’s opening range (and likely action from the big stack, Flash) gives poor equity and puts me in difficult spots. A defend should be with suited connectors, broadways or pairs — not 9‑4o — and a bluff 3‑bet from the SB here is too risky versus these stacks. Fold and preserve my deep stack for better opportunities.

I am in the Big Blind with 9d 6s. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button, and Minni has folded. Elon has a relatively short stack of 5,250 compared to the starting stacks. While 96o is a weak hand, I am getting 2.25:1 odds to see a flop. However, Elon has been playing somewhat aggressively, and my hand is easily dominated and plays poorly out of position. Given the stack depths and the fact that I've been involved in some significant pots recently, I will choose to fold this marginal hand and wait for a better spot to utilize my chip lead.

On the button with 3♥7♦ (very weak, poor equity and playability). Facing two opponents (SB Flash 26k, BB Elon 5.6k) and likely to be called or 3‑bet if I try a light open, this is not a spot to invest chips. Calling 100 into 150 multiway with this hand is a marginal +EV at best and will put me in difficult postflop spots. I’ll preserve my stack and fold preflop.

I am in the Small Blind with 3s 6h, which is an extremely weak hand. Minni (Button) has already folded. Although the cost to complete is only 50 chips, 36o has very poor playability and realization of equity. Elon (Big Blind) has a relatively short stack (56BB) and has shown aggression in previous hands. Raising or calling would likely put me in a difficult spot out of position. Folding is the most disciplined play to avoid unnecessary losses with trash cards.

I am on the button with 76s, a strong speculative hand. With both Elon and Minni relatively short-stacked (57 and 80 big blinds respectively), this hand plays well if I can realize its equity or take the pot down post-flop. I will raise to 300 to apply pressure and take control of the hand.

J3 suited is a speculative hand at best, with very low playability and equity against a button raise from Flash, who has shown aggression in previous hands. I'm out of position in the small blind, facing a call of 250 into a 450 pot (roughly 1.8:1 odds), but my hand doesn't connect well enough to justify entering OOP. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. Effective stack is 5700, and risking it here with trash is -EV.

I’ll defend the big blind and call to 300 total. J♦7♥ is marginal but the button’s 3bb open is wide; calling costs 200 more into a 450 pot (≈30% pot-odds) so defending is reasonable. I don’t want to 3-bet OOP with a weak kicker — a min-raise to 500 won’t fold enough hands and a larger 3-bet creates tough postflop spots deep-stacked. Call to see a flop, proceed cautiously postflop and fold to strong aggression if I miss.

Flop A♦-5♦-8♦ with J♦7♥: I have one diamond (J♦) for a strong 4-flush (9 outs) but no pair. I’m out of position to the BTN opener who has a wide raising range and can easily have an ace or made diamond(s). Check to control the pot and realise my equity rather than bloating the pot OOP. Numerically I have ~19% to hit on the turn and ~35% to hit by the river, so I’ll call reasonable small continuation bets (roughly ≤ ~350 into this 650 pot) but fold to pot-sized or larger bets or big raises. I won’t lead or bluff-raise here; check and plan to call small bets and pursue the draw, fold to heavy aggression, and continue aggressively only if I hit.

I am on the button with 7c6c. The flop is Ad5d8d, which is a monotone board. I have a gutshot straight draw (needing a 4 or 9) but no diamonds in my hand, meaning I have very little equity if my opponent has even a single diamond or an Ace. Minni checked, which could be a trap or a weak hand. However, given the board texture, a continuation bet here is unlikely to fold out any Ace or any diamond draw. Checking back is the safest play to see a free turn card and avoid bloating the pot with a weak hand on a dangerous board.

On the turn (6♦) my J♦ completes a J-high diamond flush (my hand J♦7♥). The board A♦-5♦-8♦-6♦ is highly drawy so many of the Button’s wide preflop raising combos contain diamonds — but the fact he checked the flop makes premium diamond combos (K♦/Q♦) somewhat less likely. I’m very likely ahead of much of his range (Ax, mid pairs, single diamonds below J♦), so I want to extract value now and not give him a free river that could pair the board or bring a higher diamond. Betting also prices worse diamonds and ace/paired hands to call rather than seeing a free river or turning the initiative over OOP.

Size: I’ll bet a medium-to-large value bet (400 into 650, ~60% pot). That’s big enough to get calls from worse hands and charge hands that want to see a river, but small enough that a large check-raise from a dominating diamond (K♦/Q♦) can be folded without committing my stack. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the river and extract more when appropriate; if he raises big I’ll assume a dominating diamond and fold to over-aggression.

The board now has four diamonds, and I do not have a diamond in my hand. Although I turned a pair of sixes, any single diamond from my opponent makes a flush, and an Ace is also likely in her range. Minni's lead after checking the flop suggests she either has a diamond or is representing one. Since I have zero equity against a flush and am behind any Ace or better pair, calling is not profitable. I will fold.

3-handed on the button with T6o, a speculative hand with decent high card value and straight potential. Button ranges are very wide preflop in short-handed play, and this is a standard open-raise. Raising to 300 (3x the BB) is standard sizing to build the pot, steal blinds, or play postflop from position. Limping is too passive, folding too tight given position and dynamics. Opponents have shown calling tendencies in past hands, but position allows profitable play.

Elon (button) opened to 300. I’m in the small blind with T♣5♣. The pot is 450 and it costs me 250 to call, so I need ~250/(450+250)=35.7% equity to make a cold call immediately profitable. This hand is low and frequently dominated by a button opener, with very poor post‑flop playability out of position. If the big blind calls it becomes a multiway pot which further reduces my equity and playing options. A light 3‑bet/raise with T5♣ would be thin and easily countered by either opponent. With a healthy 84 BB stack I can wait for a better spot. Fold and conserve chips.

I am in the Big Blind with Jd4d. Elon raised to 300 from the Button. The pot is currently 450, and it costs me 200 more to call. I am getting 2.25:1 on a call. J4 suited is a weak hand, but Elon is on the button and has been aggressive with a wide range of hands. My hand has some playability due to being suited. Given the stack sizes (Elon has only 5400 chips left), I should be cautious about playing a massive pot, but calling 200 to see a flop with a suited hand in position vs the button is generally acceptable in a 3-handed game. However, J4s is right at the bottom of the range. Looking at previous hands, Elon is quite active. I will call to see a flop.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack of Diamonds and Four of Diamonds. The flop is 8h-Kh-As. This is a very coordinated board that hits a Button open range quite hard, especially since Elon has been active. I have completely missed this flop with no pair and no real draw. I am out of position against Elon. My plan is to check and fold to any significant bet, as there are very few turns that improve my hand enough to continue against an aggressive opponent.

Flop board As Kh 8h is a strong, coordinated board with top pair potential (A or K), flush draws (hearts), and straight draws (QJ, T9). Flash's check from BB after flat-calling my button open suggests weakness, as strong hands might lead or check-raise. My button raising range is polarized: value (AJ+, KQ) that hits this hard, and bluffs (suited connectors, Ax suited) that can have draws. T6o is pure air but fits bluff profile perfectly. In position, this is a standard c-bet spot to deny equity to his wide calling range (pocket pairs below 88, weaker Ax, suited broadways, draws), build pot when ahead, or take it down outright. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws/improvers and looks weak. Sizing 400 (~60% pot) balances value and bluff frequency, pressuring floats while keeping SPR manageable (~10:1 effective). Flash's history shows folding to postflop aggression often (e.g., Hands 4,16,18), supporting bluff success.

I am in the Big Blind with Jd4d. The flop is 8h-Kh-As, which is extremely favorable for the pre-flop raiser (Elon on the button), who is likely to have many Ax, Kx, and Broadway hands in his range. I have completely missed this flop and have no direct draws or equity to continue. Elon has bet 400 into a 650 pot. Continuing here against an aggressive opponent with essentially air on a board that hits his range perfectly is a losing play. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Fold. 3♠2♦ offsuit is effectively the worst non-folding button open — almost no playability (no suitedness, very few straight possibilities) and poor equity against the wide ranges both blinds will defend with. Flash (SB) is very deep and has shown a lot of preflop aggression; Elon (BB) is ~60bb and will defend or 3-bet with a wide range. Calling 100 to see a flop is a weak, marginal play and raising to 200 as a steal is likely to get called or 3-bet, putting me in a difficult spot out of fold/commit for a hand with almost no postflop equity. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot.

I am in the Small Blind with 84o. This is a very weak starting hand. Even though it has folded to me and I only have 50 more to call to see a flop against Elon in the Big Blind, the hand has very poor playability and equity. Folding is the most disciplined play to avoid being out of position with a marginal hand against a competent opponent.

I am on the button with 2s6h, a very weak hand. In a 3-handed game, I should be looking for spots to apply pressure, but 2-6 offsuit is at the bottom of the range and generally not worth playing even from the button, especially since the blinds have been somewhat active. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

AQo is a premium hand from the small blind facing only the big blind after button folds. Standard play is to raise for value and fold equity, aiming to steal the dead money in the pot or get called by worse. Raising to 3x the big blind (300 total) is a balanced size that I've used successfully in similar spots in prior hands. Minni's calling range is unknown but not too tight based on history; this size pressures her effectively while keeping pot manageable OOP with my 60bb stack.

Elon opened from the small blind to 300 and I’m in the big blind with K♦8♠ — a marginal but playable hand heads-up. Calling 200 more (total commitment 300) gives me pot odds of 200/(400+200) ≈ 33%, and K8o has reasonable equity vs a wide SB opening range. I don’t want to 3-bet unnecessarily because I’ll be out of position postflop and Elon has shown frequent aggression; a small raise here would often get called and leave me OOP with a mediocre hand. Plan: call to see a flop, proceed cautiously (look for top pair, good draws or a favorable board texture) and fold to heavy aggression when I miss.

Preflop AQs (wait, Qs Ac) raised as a strong SB steal/valor hand, called by BB. Flop 678 rainbow is extremely draw-heavy and connected, smashing BB wide defending range (suited connectors, pairs like 77-99 overpairs rare but present, 56/79/59 str8 draws, sets). My AQo is pure Ace-high air: no pair, no draw, just overcards/blockers. Equity ~25% vs her range. OOP first to act, c-betting air here is poor: high fold equity but risks raise/call by better and builds pot OOP with no equity. Check preserves stack, allows equity realization if checked behind (Ace-high live often), easy fold to bet. Balanced SB range checks sufficient air/draws here postflop. Long-term EV maximized by check-fold.

Flop: 6♦ 7♠ 8♣. I hold K♦8♠ — middle pair with a good kicker heads-up vs the small‑blind opener. Elon checked the flop after being the preflop aggressor, which can be weakness or pot‑control with a range that includes lots of single‑card straight draws (5x/9x), overcards, and some pairs. The board is very connected (lots of 5/9 straight outs) but there are no flush draws, so I want to charge those draws and get value from worse pairs/overcards. I’ll make a value/protection c‑bet of 350 into the 600 pot (roughly 1/2–2/3 pot) — large enough to deny correct pot odds to many draws but not so large that I’m forced into committing with just middle pair if he shows heavy strength. Plan: if he folds I win the pot; if he calls I re‑evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously; if he check‑raises big I give credit to trips/two‑pair/monster draws and fold.

Preflop raised AQo from SB as a steal/value hand, standard sizing. Flop 678 rainbow is a disaster: highly connected board crushing BB calling range (straight draws like 54/59/9T, pairs 66-99, two-pair combos). My AcQs is pure Ace-high bluff-catcher with ~25% equity at best (live Ace, Queen overcard). Checked OOP as planned to pot control and induce bluffs/checks. Minni's small 350 bet (half-pot) into 650 smells like thin value (e.g., 9x, underpair with draw) or probe/semi-bluff, but given her range hits this hard, folding air is mandatory to avoid tough turn OOP with bloated pot (1300+). Raising has reverse fold equity (she shoves better hands) and no showdown. Calling realizes minimal equity while risking stack on turn/river where board worsens. History shows Minni calls stations but bets for value here; preserve 5800 stack (~58bb) for premium spots over 150 hands. Clear fold.

Preflop on the button in 3-handed play, SB has folded, facing BB's 100. Hand is 3d4c, offsuit low connectors with poor playability and low equity against a calling range. While button opens can be wide vs BB (40-50% range), 34o is too weak even for steals given opponents' tendencies to defend and my 58BB stack. Folding preserves stack for better spots, avoiding OOP postflop domination.

I’m in the small blind (effectively heads-up now after the button folded) with K♠7♦. Pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 to see the flop, but K7o is a hand I want to play proactively heads-up. Raising to the 2BB standard (200 total) achieves several goals: takes the initiative, applies fold equity against the big blind’s wide defend range, and gives me position postflop (I’ll act last after the flop). The raise size is small relative to stacks so I’m not committing much (8700 stack) but I price in lots of weaker hands to fold and let me c-bet favorable flops. Folding is too tight and calling is passive — it lets the BB see cheap flops and play their range in position. I’ll raise to 200 and proceed to continuation-bet or pot-control depending on the flop texture and opponent reaction.

I am in the Big Blind with 6d 5s, a small suited-gapper. Minni has raised to 200 from the Small Blind, which is a minimum raise. Elon has already folded. I only need to call 100 more into a pot that will be 400. This gives me 4:1 pot odds to see a flop. While the hand is weak, the price is very attractive to defend in a blind-vs-blind situation, and I have a significant stack advantage over both opponents. I will call to see the flop.

I was the preflop aggressor and I still have position, so I should continue to apply pressure with a standard c-bet. The flop (6c Qd 8d) is relatively dry versus a calling BB range — it misses a lot of hands and gives me fold equity. I have K7o (only king-high and a backdoor diamond), so I don’t want to bloat the pot but do want to take the initiative and fold out hands with no pair or weak pairs. A half-pot bet (~200 into 400) balances fold equity and pot control: if called I can comfortably fold to a big raise on the turn or proceed cautiously; if I get the pot I win a healthy chunk. I’ll bet 200 and re-evaluate based on Flash’s response.

I have bottom pair (Sixes) on a Queen-high board. In a blind-versus-blind pot, Minni's lead of 200 into 400 is a standard continuation bet that can represent a wide range of hands, including air or draws. With 3:1 pot odds, bottom pair is a mandatory defend to avoid being exploited by small bets. I will call to see how the turn develops, looking to improve to two pair or trips, or to reach showdown cheaply if the board texture remains relatively static.

Turn brings 7♥ and I now have second pair (7♦7♥) with a K kicker. Pot is 800 and I’m in position; Flash called my flop c-bet so his range contains top pair (Qx/8x), middle/weak pairs (6x), broadway overcards (Kx/Ax), and diamond draws. Betting here would extract value from some worse hands and charge draws, but it also bloats the pot and commits me against the portion of his range that already beats me (Q/8) or that can raise as protection. I prefer pot control and to keep his bluffs/draws in the hand since I can comfortably call a small turn bet in position. I’ll check to induce a bet and to see a river cheaply; plan is to call small-to-medium bets (thin value or draw bets) and fold to large aggression. Action: check.

On the turn, I have improved significantly. I have bottom pair (sixes) and an open-ended straight draw (any 4 or 9). Minni's check suggests she might be slowing down with a marginal hand, a draw, or giving up on a flop bluff. While I could bet to put pressure on her, checking back is a safer way to ensure I realize my equity and see the river for free. If I hit my straight, I can value bet. If I miss, I might still win with my pair or try a bluff depending on the river card and her action.

I have K♠7♦ — second pair (7s) with a good kicker on 6♣ Q♦ 8♦ 7♥ 4♠. Flash called my flop c-bet then checked the turn and now checked the river as well. His line looks like Qx/8x, some pairs, missed diamond draws or overcards. Betting for thin value (⅓–½ pot) would get called by most hands that beat me (Q/8/overpairs) and only fold out a few worse hands; it also opens me up to a check-raise bluff or bloating the pot out of position. Given pot control, the line I took on the turn (check to see a cheap river) and the low expected incremental EV from a river stab, the +EV play is to check and take showdown.

I have completed a straight on the river (4-5-6-7-8). Minni checked the turn and the river, which suggests she likely has a marginal hand like a Queen or a pair of eights, or perhaps a missed draw. While a 9 would make a higher straight, her passivity on the river makes it less likely she holds one. I will bet 500 for value, as I expect to be called by a Queen or other one-pair hands that she might want to take to showdown.

Board: 6c Qd 8d 7h 4s. I hold K♠7♦ — second pair with a strong kicker. Flash called my flop c-bet and then both checked the turn; his river stab (500 into a 1300 pot) is a small-to-medium sizing that can include thin value (Qx/8x) but also missed hands/overcards and occasional bluffs. Pot odds on a 500 call into the 1300 pot require ~28% equity. My pair of 7s has reasonable showdown equity versus a wide calling range and will beat most bluffs and many weaker pairs; folding here would give up too much equity against his non-value hands. Raising would be a thin, risky bluff that can be crushed by his value hands and unnecessarily bloats the pot given stack depths. Consistent with my earlier plan to call small-to-medium river bets for showdown, the +EV play is to call and see a showdown.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed pot — 7d9c is a playable, positionally advantaged hand and we should open fairly wide. Pot is 150 and the call is 100; min-raise is 200. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x the big blind). This sizing is standard at this table: it applies pressure to the SB, gives us good fold equity, and keeps the pot manageable versus short-stacked Elon (5700) who can shove — a shove from him I can comfortably fold to. If called, I’ll use my positional advantage to c-bet favorable flops and realize equity; if everyone folds I pick up the blinds. Overall this raise maximizes our expected value from this spot.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Ten of clubs, which is a premium starting hand in a 3-handed game. Minni has opened from the Button, and with both opponents sitting on relatively shallow stacks (around 60-75 big blinds), I want to play a larger pot and take the initiative. Raising (3-betting) allows me to isolate the Button or win the pot immediately if everyone folds. A sizing of 1000 is standard to put pressure on the opener while building a pot that suits the strength of my hand.

52o is one of the worst hands in poker, with no straight or flush potential, dominated by any raising range. Button open raise followed by SB 3-bet indicates strength from both opponents. Calling 900 into 1400 pot requires ~39% equity, but 52o has <30% against typical 3-bet ranges. Effective stacks are shallow relative to pot; folding preserves stack for better spots.

I opened the button to 300 with 7♦9♣ which is fine as an exploitative button open. Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1,000 and Elon folded. The pot is ~1,400 and I must call an extra 700 (to put 1,000 total in) — that requires roughly 700/(1,400+700) ≈ 33% equity to be correct. 7♠9♣ offsuit plays poorly vs a standard SB 3‑bet range (likely weighted to pairs, Ax and broadway hands) and won’t have the needed equity or reliable postflop realization against that range even though I’d have position. 4‑betting as a bluff is too high-variance here (I don’t have good blockers) and calling gives poor implied odds against a likely strong or polarized range. Preserve the stack and wait for a better, more favorable spot — fold.

I am on the button with 8h 5c, which is a very weak starting hand. With both Elon and Minni relatively short-stacked (56 BB and 74 BB respectively) and having shown active play in previous hands, attempting a steal with garbage is suboptimal. The most disciplined and standard play here is to fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my chip lead.

Preflop in small blind with 3h4c offsuit, a very weak hand with poor playability and low equity. Flash has folded on the button, leaving heads-up vs Minni in the big blind who defends wide. Completing from SB puts me out of position postflop with trash, risking building a pot OOP on future streets without initiative. Pot odds are about 3:1 (150:50), but the hand lacks implied odds or fold equity for a raise. Folding the SB loses only 50 chips but preserves stack for better spots; playing weak hands OOP leads to losses long-term. Standard fold to maximize EV over 150 hands.

3c8s is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability. Even on the button in a 3-handed game, the standard opening range includes stronger speculative hands. Opponents have large stacks (Minni 75BB, Flash 266BB) and have defended raises frequently in history. Calling 100 into 150 pot offers poor implied odds post-flop in position but with a hand that rarely improves well. Raising risks 300+ to win 150 uncalled, but fold equity is uncertain given opponents' calling tendencies. Best to fold and wait for a better spot to maximize long-term stack growth.

We're heads-up (Elon folded) so I'm the small blind against Flash's big blind. I have Jd3h — a weak, non-pair, non-suited hand, but in heads-up play the SB can open a very wide range and retains positional advantage on all post‑flop streets. Pot is 150 and it only costs 50 to complete; the min‑raise is to 200. I prefer to apply pressure with a standard small open here rather than just limp: a min‑raise to 200 (committing 200 total) buys fold equity vs Flash's wide defending range, keeps the initiative, and when called I still have position postflop to play cheaply or seize pots. If Flash 3‑bets, J3o is easily foldable and the loss will be small relative to my stack. Given Flash's history of defending and calling wide, raising the min is the +EV, straightforward play.

I am in the Big Blind with Kc 8c. Minni, in the Small Blind, has raised to 200, making it 100 more for me to call into a pot of 300. This is a very favorable price (3-to-1 odds) and K8s is a strong hand in a heads-up blind-versus-blind situation. While I could consider a 3-bet to put pressure on Minni's likely wide opening range, calling allows me to play a pot in position with a hand that flops well. I'll take the call and see a flop.

Heads-up I was the preflop aggressor with a very wide opening range. On 4d Th 4c I hold Jd3h — no pair and no real draw, but I have position and decent fold equity as the preflop raiser. Pot is 400 and a small continuation bet (~1/2 pot) applies pressure to many of Flash’s wide calling hands (overcards, weak Broadway, small pairs) while keeping the price reasonable relative to my 7,400 stack. I plan to bet 200 to take the initiative: fold to a strong check‑raise (I’ll save chips with this airball hand), call single raises rarely if pricing is correct, and if called proceed cautiously on the turn (looking to improve to a pair or to take advantage of further aggression).

I am in position on a paired flop of 4-T-4. Minni has lead out with a half-pot bet of 200. My hand, Kc 8c, is currently King-high but has two overcards and a backdoor flush draw. Given the pot odds (4-to-1) and the fact that Minni could be continuation betting with a wide range of hands that I currently beat or have decent equity against, a call is the most appropriate action. Raising here is an option but might bloat the pot unnecessarily with only King-high. I'll call to see how the turn develops.

I was the preflop aggressor and c‑bet the flop with a very wide range. On 4d Th 4c 2s my Jd3h has no real showdown value, but I retain position and can apply pressure to a lot of Flash’s calling hands (overcards, weak broadways, floats). The flop bet was called; the turn is a fairly harmless low card that doesn’t help many of Villain’s range. I want to take the pot now with a small-sized second barrel rather than give up initiative. I’ll fire ~1/3–ish pot (300 chips more) to maximize fold equity while keeping the pot controllable — that means I will have committed 700 total so far (200 preflop + 200 flop + 300 turn). Plan: fold to a sizeable check‑raise, and if called I’ll re-evaluate on the river (likely pot‑control/check a blank).

On the turn of 4d-Th-4c-2s, Minni has increased her betting size to 700 into a 1500 pot. This 'double barrel' on a relatively dry, paired board suggests she either has a Ten, a pocket pair, or is continuing a bluff with high equity. My hand, King-high, has not improved and lacks significant draws other than the backdoor clubs which didn't materialize. While I have position, calling 700 with only King-high against a player who is showing aggression on multiple streets is a losing play in the long run. I don't have enough equity to justify the call, and raising as a bluff is risky given the board texture. I will fold.

I’m on the button with 9h8h — a suited connector with good playability in position. Pot is 150 and it’s 100 to call (min-raise to 200). I’ll open to 300 (≈3x BB), a standard button open. This sizing takes the initiative, applies pressure to the blinds, and keeps the pot controllable if called. 9h8h plays very well postflop in position (straight/flush potential) so I can continue aggressively or realize equity cheaply. If the deep SB 3-bets big I’ll respect a strong range and proceed cautiously; versus Elon (the shorter BB) I’ll adjust based on his response. Overall, raising now is the +EV line.

I am in the Small Blind with 8s 7d. Minni (the Button) has opened to 300. While 87o is a semi-connected hand, it performs poorly out of position, especially against a Button opening range which can be quite wide but still stronger than this hand. With the current stack depths (both opponents are significantly shorter than me) and the lack of high-card value or suit connectivity, folding is the most disciplined and standard play to avoid difficult spots out of position.

Facing a button raise to 300 (3bb) after SB fold. In BB with K7s: suited king-high hand with good playability and a strong blocker. Pot odds excellent (calling 200 into 450, ~31% equity required). Button ranges are wide in this spot based on Minni's history of frequent raising from late position. Calling to realize equity OOP is standard; hand flops decent equity often (top pair potential, straights, flush draws). Raising too thin without position or fold equity justification. Folding too tight.

Flop completely misses my K7s: no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw, only weak backdoors and king-high. As OOP in BB after calling pre-flop raise, standard to check first. Button (Minni) likely continuation bets wide on this Q-T-2 rainbow-ish board with straight draw potential. Plan to fold to most bets, as hand has poor equity and playability going forward. Checking preserves stack and avoids bluffing OOP with marginal hand. Pre-flop call was for equity realization, but flop didn't connect.

I opened preflop with 9♥8♥ and am acting last on the flop (T♠ 2♥ Q♦). I have no pair but I have backdoor hearts and four J outs to make a straight by the river — overall limited showdown value but decent semi-bluff equity. Elon checked, which often denotes a wide/calling or marginal range after defending the BB. Betting here applies pressure to many of his missed overcards and small pairs, preserves fold equity, and takes advantage of position. I’ll make a standard half‑pot continuation bet (≈325 into 650): large enough to fold out worse hands, small enough to keep the pot controllable if called. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if called I’ll continue on favorable cards (J, hearts, or safe blanks) and give up to heavy pot‑sized raises since my raw equity is limited. Against a modest call I can still realize equity in position; against big resistance I’ll avoid bloating the pot with just backdoor outs.

Flop Ts2hQd completely misses Kd7s: no pair, no straight draw (K7 gutshot but weak), no flush draw, pure king-high bluff catcher at best. OOP after check-call pre, facing ~1/3 pot cbet from button (Minni), who ranges wide in position with frequent small bets historically (e.g., Hand 20, 26, etc.). Pot odds ~3:1 (325 to call into 1300) require ~25% equity, but Kd7s has poor playability forward—no initiative, reverse implied odds if hit king, blocked from strong Kx. Calling commits to further tough spots OOP vs wide range favoring Qx/draws/overpairs. Raising as bluff thin without fold equity or position (stack ~53bb effective). Folding preserves stack for better spots; preflop call realized no equity. Standard discipline vs button cbet.

I am on the button with 9c 5s, which is a very weak hand. While the button is a good position for stealing blinds, 9-5 offsuit is too low in my opening range to justify a raise here, especially since both opponents in the blinds have shown willingness to play back. Folding is the standard disciplined play.

Button folded, leaving heads-up vs BB with 82BB stack. Ah8c is a strong SB raising hand: ace-high blocker reduces villain's premium hands, good postflop playability in position. Standard steal raise ~3x BB to 300 to apply pressure on Minni's wide defending range observed in history. Steal has good fold equity, and if called, continue favorably. Completing is too passive given position and hand strength.

Elon (SB) opened to 300 after the button folded. I’m in the big blind with 9♠5♦ — a weak, non-blocker hand heads-up and effectively out of position postflop. A 3‑bet OOP with this holding is thin: a small 3‑bet will fold only the worst of his range and get called by hands that dominate me, while a larger 3‑bet needlessly bloats the pot out of position. Calling keeps the pot controlled, buys a cheap look at the flop (calling 200 into a ~400 pot requires ~33% equity), and preserves flexibility — I can fold to heavy action on dry boards or continue aggressively if I pair or pick up a draw. With these considerations I’ll defend the blind and call to see the flop.

Preflop: Raised as SB steal with A8o, ace blocker and playability, got called by BB Minni who defends wide based on history. Flop: Qd6s4h is a good c-bet spot OOP - board is somewhat coordinated with possible straight draws (53, 75) and backdoor flush, but low connectivity overall. No strong made hands likely in her calling range (misses most Ax, pairs). Ace-high has some showdown value and blocks her top Ax; pure bluff with fold equity against her marginal floats. Standard 2/3 pot c-bet ~400 denies equity, builds pot if strong, applies pressure given stacks (50BB eff). If called, proceed cautiously turn with backdoor equity.

I’m OOP with 9♠5♦ on 4♥-6♠-Q♦ facing Elon’s 400 into a 1000 pot. I have no pair, no meaningful draw and only 6 outs to pair by the river (≈24% to improve), which is only marginally above the break‑even equity for a call (400 into a 1800 final pot → ≈22.2%). Out of position I’ll be hard‑pressed to realize that equity and I’m often dominated by Elon’s c‑betting range (Qs, pairs, overpairs, some draws). A bluff‑raise here would bloat the pot with very little fold equity and poor playability post‑raise. Folding preserves my stack for better spots and avoids getting turned into a bluff catcher OOP on later streets. Therefore I fold.

Preflop on the button with 2d6c, a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and low card value. No suitedness or playability justifies entering the pot, especially with a to-call of 100 into a small pot. Opponents have deep stacks (Minni 80BB, Flash 262BB), and history shows frequent aggression from all, but this hand has zero equity against raises. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Goal is long-term stack maximization over 150 hands; speculative calls here lead to tough postflop situations.

7♥3♦ is a very weak hand, but this is heads-up vs the big blind and I have positional advantage post-flop (SB acts last after the flop). The cost to continue is only 50 chips (bring my total commitment to 100) into a 150-chip pot — cheap to see a flop and realize equity. Flash (BB) is a deep, often wide defender, so raising here with junk just bloats the pot and can get me 3-bet or played off. I’ll defend by calling and see a flop; plan to fold to significant aggression if I miss, and only continue when I pair or have a good draw.

I have Ten-Eight of diamonds in the Big Blind. Elon has folded from the button, and Minni has completed the Small Blind. With a suited hand that plays well in position post-flop, I am happy to take a free flop. There is no significant reason to raise and inflate the pot at this stage, so I will check and see the flop.

I flopped two pair (7s and 3s) on J♣ 3♣ 7♦ — a very strong hand on a relatively dry board. Flash checked preflop and will have a wide range including many single-pair hands (Jx, 7x, weaker pairs and some overcards), so I want to extract value while charging the few club-backdoor/higher-card draws. A half-pot lead gives good price to call for worse one-pair hands and keeps the pot manageable if I face resistance from sets. I’ll bet a half pot (100) to get value and deny a free card; if he raises large I’ll strongly consider he has a set or a very strong jack and will re-evaluate (likely folding to a big shove, calling a small raise depending on sizing).

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Eight of diamonds. The flop is Jack-Trey-Seven with one diamond. Minni, in the Small Blind, leads with a small bet of 100 into a 200 pot. I have two overcards to the 3 and 7, and a backdoor straight draw. While I don't have a made hand, the price is very attractive (100 to call for a 300 pot). Minni's lead into a limped pot doesn't necessarily represent a Jack. I can call here to see if I improve on the turn or if Minni checks, giving me a chance to take the pot later.

I have two pair (7s and 3s) on Jc 3c 7d 5c. The 5c brings a third club, so the main hands that now beat me are made flushes (villain holding two clubs) and sets (33 or 77). However, Flash called my half-pot flop bet with a wide range that is largely dominated by single-pair hands (Jx, 7x, 3x) and some one-club/one-card draws — true two-club flush combos are relatively uncommon. I'm ahead versus most of his calling range and want to extract value while keeping the pot from ballooning if I run into a flush or set. With position I can still control the hand if I face aggression. I'll make a value/protection bet of roughly half the pot (200). Plan: if he calls I go to the river and re-evaluate; if he raises small I may call to evaluate the river; if he raises big or shoves I’ll assume he likely has a flush or set and fold.

On the turn, the board is Jack of Clubs, Trey of Clubs, Seven of Diamonds, and Five of Clubs. I am holding Eight of Diamonds and Ten of Diamonds. Minni has led for a small bet of 200 into a pot of 400. Currently, I have no made hand, but I have a backdoor diamond draw (only one on board) and two overcards to the lower board cards. However, the 5 of clubs completes some possible draws and adds a third club to the board. Minni's persistent small betting suggests either a marginal made hand (like a 7 or a 5) or a draw. Given the pot odds (200 to call 600), I have the price to see a river, where I might hit a Ten or an Eight for top pair/good pair, or potentially represent a hand if the board provides a scare card. I will call the 200.

I hold two pair (7s & 3s) on Jc 3c 7d 5c 9d. The river missed the backdoor club but the hands that realistically beat me are sets/trips (33, 77, JJ), two‑pair combinations that include a J (J3, J7) and made two‑club flushes (if villain held two clubs). Flash checked preflop and then called a half‑pot flop bet (100) and a half‑pot turn bet (200). That line is much more consistent with a wide calling range of single‑pair hands (Jx, 7x, 3x), one‑club draws and some medium pairs — not highly indicative of a large number of made flushes or sets. Given I’m ahead of a large portion of his continuing range, I should extract value. I’ll make a value/protection bet of about half the pot (400 into 800) — large enough to charge worse one‑pair hands but small enough to keep the pot manageable vs the few hands that dominate me. Plan: if he calls we go to showdown; if he raises big or jams I’ll assume a very strong made hand (flush/trips) and fold to significant aggression (I may consider calling a small raise depending on sizing).

On the river, the board is Jack of Clubs, Trey of Clubs, Seven of Diamonds, Five of Clubs, and Nine of Diamonds. I hold Ten-Eight of diamonds, which is only Ten-high. Minni has led for a small bet on every street (flop, turn, and river). While the pot odds are 3:1 (400 to call a 1200 pot), I have no showdown value. Even if Minni is bluffing, she likely has at least an Ace-high or a small pair that beats my Ten-high. Raising as a bluff is risky because her betting line suggests a made hand, likely a Jack or a smaller pair, and I haven't shown much strength. Therefore, folding is the most prudent decision.

I’m on the button with J♣8♥ — a playable but marginal hand. With both blinds to act and strong positional advantage, I’ll open to take the initiative and apply pressure. Raising to 300 (3 BB) is a standard button open-size here: big enough to fold out some blind defence and not give good odds for speculative calls, but small relative to stacks so I’m not overcommitting with a marginal holding. Plan if action: fold to a large 3‑bet (especially from Elon with ~55BB); consider calling only versus very small 3‑bets or wide SB floats. If called, play post‑flop in position — c‑bet dry, pot‑control or check when unfavorable.

I have 4s 9c in the small blind. Minni has raised to 300 from the button. This is a very weak hand that does not play well post-flop, especially out of position against a button raiser. The pot odds are not good enough to justify a call with such a weak hand, and a 3-bet bluff is low-efficiency here given our relative stack depths and the nature of the hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Q6o is a weak offsuit hand with poor equity against Minni's button raising range, which appears wide and aggressive based on hand history (frequent opens and continuation bets). Facing a standard 3x raise from the button heads-up, the pot odds (calling 200 into 650) do not justify entering with such a dominated, unplayable hand lacking straight or flush potential. Folding preserves my 5550 stack for better spots in this long 150-hand game, avoiding speculative defense in BB that leads to difficult postflop decisions.

I am on the button with A6o. This is a standard raising hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is very large compared to the blinds and my opponents. Elon (SB) has a relatively short stack (55BB), while Minni (BB) has 85BB. I will raise to 300 to exert pressure and potentially take down the blinds or play in position with a strong ace.

86o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability, especially out of position in the SB against a Button raise. Pot odds to call 250 into 450 (about 3.2:1) are not sufficient given the range Flash raises from Button, which includes many strong and speculative hands. No position advantage post-flop, low implied odds with 5500 stack. Standard fold to avoid difficult spots OOP with trash.

I’m in the big blind with Js5h facing a button open to 300. This is a weak, offsuit, poorly-connected hand that will be dominated by a large portion of the button’s opening range (many A/K/Q/Jx and broadways). Calling 200 into a 450 pot requires about 31% equity (200/(450+200)≈30.8%), and J5o out of position is unlikely to realize that equity or be comfortably playable postflop. A minimum reraise would be a thin bluff with poor blockers and would commit stacks out of position. Against Flash’s frequent opening/aggressive tendencies I’d rather preserve chips for better spots — fold.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed pot with blinds posted (pot 150, to call 100 likely after SB limp or fold). Holding 4s3c offsuit, a marginal hand with straight potential but poor equity heads-up. However, button position allows wide opening range to steal blinds frequently. Opponents have large stacks (Minni 8450, Flash 25900), and history shows they fold often to raises. Raising to 300 (3x BB) applies fold equity, wins the pot immediately ~70-80% of time, and maintains aggression consistent with my image (frequent preflop raiser with speculative hands like 53o in past). Postflop OOP playability is decent in position if called. Folding is too tight 3-handed; limping weak. Better EV to raise than fold or limp.

Fold. K♦2♥ is a very weak, poorly playable hand from the small blind facing a button open to 300. Calling 250 to play out of position versus Elon (and possibly a deep BB caller) commits chips with weak equity and lots of dominated matchups; the effective pot odds (~36%) don’t justify seeing flops OOP with this hand. A mini 3-bet bluff is also unattractive because Flash (BB) is deep and likely to defend or squeeze, and I lack reliable blockers. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Nine of spades, which is a strong suited connector. Elon (Button) has raised to 300, and Minni has folded. Elon's stack is quite short (5,200 chips), which changes the implied odds, but with 200 more to call into a pot of 450, I am getting 2.25:1. This is a standard defend in this spot against a button open, as my hand plays well post-flop and has significant equity against a wide button opening range. Given his short stack, I will likely look for opportunities to check-shove or call down if I hit a piece of the board.

I have top pair (Nines) with a Ten kicker on a very dry board (4c 5h 9d). This is a strong hand in a blind-versus-button situation. My standard play here is to check to the pre-flop aggressor to allow him to continue with his wide opening range, which includes many hands I currently beat (overcards, smaller pairs). If Elon bets, I intend to continue, as my hand is too strong to fold and often doesn't need to turn into a bluff by check-raising immediately on such a dry texture.

Flop is 4c5h9d, giving me bottom pair (4s) with a gutshot straight draw (needing a 2 or 6 for the wheel straight, 8 clean outs). Strong hand with ~30-35% equity vs a typical BB calling range. Flash checks after calling preflop raise, showing weakness or trapping, but as the preflop aggressor in position, I should bet for value against his weaker pairs/draws/floats and to protect against overcards/draws like 67, T8, A-high. Checking back risks giving free cards to his draws or allowing bluffs. Pot 650, betting ~60% pot (400) sizes well for value, denies equity, and builds pot with my strong showdown value. Consistent with aggressive image from history (frequent flop bets after preflop raises). If raised, can reevaluate based on sizing/range.

I have top pair (Nines) with a Ten kicker on a very dry board (4c 5h 9d). Elon's continuation bet of 400 into a pot of 650 is a standard size. Against a button opening range, my hand is ahead of most of his air and smaller pairs. Given his relatively short stack, I want to keep the pot manageable and allow him to continue bluffing with overcards on later streets. Raising here might force out the hands I beat while only getting called by hands that have me crushed (overpairs or sets). Calling is the most efficient way to realize my equity and extract value.

On the turn 7h, the board becomes more coordinated, bringing possible straight draws (68, 86) and a flush draw. I still hold top pair (Nines) with a Ten kicker. My goal remains to realize my equity and keep the pot manageable against Elon, who has a relatively short stack. Checking allows me to see how Elon reacts to the board texture; if he checks back, I maintain my lead, and if he bets, I can evaluate based on sizing. Checking is the most balanced move to protect my range and induce bluffs from his overcard hands.

Turn board 7h completes some straight draws (like 56, 68) and adds a second heart for flush draws, but doesn't directly improve my bottom pair (4s pairing the 4c) significantly beyond the gutshot I had on flop (now fewer outs). However, I still have decent showdown value with bottom pair + backdoor possibilities, and the board is drawy. Flash has checked twice (flop call then check, now turn check), showing continued weakness after defending BB wide preflop. His range includes weak pairs (6x, 8x, Tx), draws (flush/ straight incomplete like 6h8h, AhX), floats, and some made hands like 99/77 but unlikely given passivity. As preflop and flop aggressor in position with an aggressive table image (frequent raises/bets from history, e.g., Hands 13,20,25,26,43,51), betting maintains pressure, gets value from his worse pairs/draws/A-high, denies equity to his draws (8 outs or less), and forces folds from improving hands or pure air. Pot 1450, my stack 4800 (good for deep play), bet ~55% pot (800) sizes well for thin value/protection without overbetting, builds pot if he has it, and SPR low post-bet (~3.5:1). Checking risks giving free river to draws or bluffs, and pot control is less important with position. High EV to bet given fold equity (~40-50% vs his range) and history of Flash folding postflop (e.g., Hands 2,4,12,16,19,43).

I have top pair with a Ten kicker on a board that is becoming more coordinated (4c 5h 9d 7h). Elon is continuing his aggression from the button. At this point, my hand is too strong to fold to a half-pot bet, as I beat all his bluffs and some smaller pairs he might be value-betting. However, raising would likely only get action from hands that have me beat (overpairs, two pairs, or straights). By calling, I keep his bluffing range in and see a river with a manageable pot relative to his remaining stack. I am getting roughly 3:1 on the call, which is sufficient equity to continue.

On this river, the Jack is an overcard to my pair of Nines, and both the straight (68) and the heart flush have completed. My hand has marginal showdown value, but it is not strong enough to bet for value. Checking is the most prudent action to see how Elon, who has shown aggression on every street, proceeds. If Elon checks back, I may win against missed draws or lower pairs. If he bets, I will have to evaluate based on his sizing, but for now, checking is the standard play.

Flash has checked three streets on a coordinated board (4c5h9d7hJh) featuring straight draws (e.g., 56 for wheel, 68/78/89 for straights) and three hearts (missed flush). My bottom pair (4s) with poor kicker (3c) has decent showdown value against his BB defending range after calling preflop raise, flop c-bet (400 into ~650), and turn bet (800 into ~1450), which likely includes missed draws (6x hearts, 68, A-high floats), weaker pairs (5x, underpairs postflop), and some traps like sets but unlikely given consistent checking. Stronger hands (99, 77, Jx, straights) would often bet river themselves or raise earlier, but his passivity (three checks) suggests capping range at medium strength or air. My aggressive image (frequent pre/tpostflop aggression in history, e.g., Hands 13,20,25,26,28,43,51) induces folds pre-turn, but here continued calls show some strength; however, river check denies thin value opportunities without risking raises from better (e.g., Jx, 89). Pot 3050, my stack 4000 (SPR ~1.3), betting risks bloating pot OOP-no, IP but committing stack thinly vs worse (which folds) or better (raises/calls). High EV to check behind, realize equity (~50-60% vs his capped range), win unimproved pots, and preserve stack for better spots in 3-handed dynamic where I've built image. Consistent with selective aggression: bet flop/turn for value/protection, check river for pot control/SDV.

I’m on the button with Q♥ T♦ — a playable, positionally advantaged hand. Stacks: I’m ~84BB, SB (Flash) is deep and BB (Elon) is ~39BB. Standard play is to open-raise and take the initiative: this extracts fold equity, isolates the BB more often, and gives post-flop positional leverage. I’ll raise to 300 (3x BB), a comfortable, table-consistent size that keeps pots manageable against the shortish BB. Plan: if called I’ll c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously on A/K/J-high boards; fold to a large, polarized 3-bet from Elon given my hand and his stack size.

I am in the Small Blind with 9s 8s, a strong suited connector that performs well in multi-way pots and has good post-flop playability. Minni's 3x open from the Button is standard, and while I could consider a 3-bet to isolate, a call is well-justified given my stack size and the odds. I need to be mindful of Elon in the Big Blind, who has a shorter stack (39 BB) and might look to squeeze, but 98s is strong enough to continue with.

T5o is a marginal hand at best, offsuit with poor connectivity and low high-card strength. Facing a button raise from Minni (loose-aggressive based on history) and a Small Blind call from Flash (defensive stack), the range they're playing is strong enough that my equity is low (~30-35% vs typical ranges). Pot odds are 700:200 (~3:1), but implied odds are limited OOP with 39bb stack, and multiway pot postflop with this hand is disastrous due to reverse domination and poor playability. Folding preserves stack for premium spots; no history justifies a loose defend here.

On this flop of 5h-6s-Th, I have an open-ended straight draw (any 7 or 9 gives me a straight). This is a strong drawing hand. The pot is currently 700 chips. Being first to act post-flop against the original raiser (Minni) and with a fairly shallow-stacked Elon already folded, I will check to see how Minni acts. This allows me to either call a bet to see the turn or potentially check-raise if I feel she is continuation-betting light, though given her stack size of roughly 81 BB, I prefer seeing a cheap turn.

I raised preflop from the button with Q♦T♦ (QhTd) and the SB (Flash) called. Flop is 5h-6s-Th: I have top pair with a strong queen kicker and positional advantage. The board is somewhat coordinated (some straight combos like 7-8 and small connectors), but Flash has shown a tendency to call and check rather than lead or polarize. Standard and +EV play is to c-bet for value and protection — charge draws and worse tens/pairs and fold out overcards that missed. Pot is 700; I’ll size to ~55% to get value without overcommitting vs deeper caller. If he check-calls I can continue selectively; if he check-raises big I’ll re-evaluate (he’s likely strong: sets/two-pair), and proceed cautiously. Bet 400 to extract value and deny equity.

On the flop of 5h-6s-Th, I hold 9s 8s, which gives me a gutshot straight draw (needing a 7 to make 5-6-7-8-9 or 6-7-8-9-10) and a backdoor spade flush draw. Minni's continuation bet of 400 into a pot of 700 is a standard size. Given my stack depth and the potential implied odds if I hit my straight, calling to see the turn is the most profitable play. I am getting roughly 2.75:1 on a call, and with overcards that might be clean and the straight draw, I have enough equity to continue.

On the turn, the Ace of Spades (As) has significantly improved my hand's potential by giving me a flush draw to go along with my gutshot straight draw (needing a 7). The Ace is also a card that very likely hits Minni's range as the pre-flop aggressor. Being out of position, I will check to see how Minni reacts to the Ace. If she bets, I have plenty of equity to call with 12 clean outs (9 spades and 3 additional 7s, excluding the 7h which might complete a heart flush if one were possible, though only two hearts are on board). Checking allows me to realize my equity or potentially check-raise if I believe she is betting thin.

I have top pair (T) with a good Q kicker on an Ace‑high turn (5h 6s Th As). Flash called my flop c‑bet and now checks the turn — a line consistent with a wide calling range (weaker tens, pocket pairs, and straight draws) and occasional slowplayed A or set. Checking gives free cards to the straight draws and surrenders initiative; betting charges draws and extracts value from worse tens and hands that will call small. I’ll make a pot‑control/value bet (about half the pot) so I don’t overcommit if he shows strength. If he check‑raises big I’ll presume he has an Ace or very strong made hand and fold/re‑evaluate; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the river based on the card and his tendencies.

On the turn, the Ace of Spades (As) has provided me with a flush draw to go along with my gutshot straight draw (needing a 7). I have approximately 11 clean outs (8 spades and 3 additional sevens, since the 7s is counted in the flush outs). The pot is 2250 and I need to call 750, giving me 3:1 odds (25%). My direct equity is approximately 24%, making this a call based on direct odds and very profitable when considering implied odds, as Minni is likely to pay off a bet on the river if I hit, given that the Ace likely improved her range. I will call and look to realize my equity on the river.

I have 9-high and have missed both my open-ended straight draw and my flush draw. The board now contains an Ace and a Queen, both of which are very likely to have improved Minni's range, as she has been the aggressor throughout the hand. Given that I have no showdown value and Minni is likely committed to the pot with at least top pair, attempting a bluff is low-frequency and risky. I will check and fold to any bet, or hope for a check-back to see if by some miracle my 9-high is good (though it almost certainly is not).

I have QhTd — river Q gives me two pair (Qs and Ts) on a 5h-6s-Th-As-Qd board. Pot is 3000 and Flash (SB) checked the river after check-calling my flop and turn bets. His line is passive and wide, so his range likely contains many worse hands (weaker tens, small pocket pairs, missed draws) that will call a value bet. Only a limited subset of hands beat me (AT/AQ, sets like AA/TT), and those combos are relatively rare. Betting for value is +EV. I’ll bet about half the pot (1500) to get called by worse tens and pairs while not overcommitting if he shows strength. If he raises big (polarized), I’ll respect that and fold; if he calls, I go to showdown and expect to be ahead most of the time.

I have 9-high and missed all my draws (double-gutshot and flush draw). Minni has been the aggressor on every street, betting into an Ace-high and Queen-high board. I have no showdown value, and a bluff is unlikely to succeed given the board texture and the line Minni has taken, which strongly suggests she has a made hand like top pair or better. Folding is the only sensible option.

I am on the button with T3o. This is a very weak hand. Given the stack sizes, especially with Elon being relatively short in the Small Blind and Minni being quite active and aggressive in the Big Blind, there is little reason to attempt a steal with such a low-equity hand. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack.

Preflop in SB vs BB, Flash folded. Hand Jd5h is offsuit, gapped, and weak with poor playability and equity realization postflop. Stack is 38.5BB, not desperate; better to fold trash and wait for better spots rather than risk chips on a marginal steal attempt against Minni who defends frequently. Preserve stack for stronger hands.

3-handed on the button, first to act pre-flop with A4o. Excellent position, ace blocker, playable hand in short-handed play. Pot is 150 with blinds posted (SB 50, BB 100), to call 100 or raise. Limping is weak; better to raise for value, fold equity, or to steal blinds. Standard sizing is 3x BB to 300 total. My stack of 3850 (38.5BB) supports aggression without overcommitting. History shows frequent button raises to 300 succeed often against Minni and Flash.

I’m in the small blind with Qc8s facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, 250 to call). Q8o is a marginal, often-dominated hand vs a BTN opening range and it plays very poorly out of position — especially with the big blind still to act. Calling requires ~36% equity just to break even preflop and commits me to difficult postflop decisions against an aggressive opener (Elon) and a live BB (Flash). A 3-bet here would be too thin (no suitedness or strong blockers) and would need fold equity I don’t have. Preserve the stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Trey offsuit. Elon, the button, has raised to 300. Although I am getting decent odds to call, T3o is a very weak hand that performs poorly out of position, especially against a shorter stack like Elon's where I won't have much room to maneuver post-flop. It's better to fold and wait for a stronger hand.

Dead hand: 2♣9♠ is one of the weakest holdings. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see a flop — that price requires ~40% equity vs the remaining ranges to be +EV right now. Against two opponents (Flash, a very loose/aggressive big-stacked SB defender, and Elon, a shorter-stacked BB who will call or shove light), my hand has very poor equity and terrible postflop playability. Calling invites multiway spots I'm dominated in; a small open-raise will frequently get defended or 3‑bet by Flash or induce a shove from Elon, leaving me in bad shape. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot on the button — fold here.

I am in the Small Blind with Ks 6c. After the Button (Minni) folds, it is a blind versus blind situation. Elon (Big Blind) has a relatively short stack of 3900 chips (39 BB). King-high is a strong hand heads-up and warrants a raise to put pressure on the Big Blind. I will raise to 300 to attempt to win the pot immediately or play a pot with a range advantage.

Preflop in the big blind facing a raise to 300 from Flash in the small blind after Minni folded. My hand is 5d2s, a complete trash hand with no high card value, no suitedness relevant here, and poor playability postflop. Stack is 3900 (39BB), but out of position with this holding against a steal raise from SB, which has a wide range but still crushes this hand. Pot odds are 200 to call into 400 (3:1), but equity is abysmal (~20-30% vs typical SB raise range). No reason to defend; folding preserves stack for better spots.

I am on the button with Q7o. While this is sometimes an opening hand in three-handed play, the stack sizes and the aggression levels of my opponents make it a marginal play. Elon in the Small Blind has a relatively short stack (38.5 BB), which is a prime size for a re-shove. Minni in the Big Blind has shown a willingness to defend and play aggressively post-flop. Given that Q7o does not play well against a raise and has poor playability post-flop, I will choose to fold and look for a better spot to utilize my chip lead.

Preflop in SB, folded to me vs BB. Hand is 84o, offsuit non-connected trash with poor playability and low equity vs calling range. Pot odds are good (50 to call into 150), but post-flop I'll be OOP with a weak hand likely dominated or drawing nothing. Standard play is to fold weak hands here to avoid tough spots. Preserve stack for better spots.

3-handed on the button with Ad6d (A6 suited), a strong opening hand due to ace-high, suited connectors potential for straights/flushes, and position advantage. Standard to open-raise from button with this range to steal blinds or build pot in position. Opponents are deep-stacked (Minni 100x BB, Flash 260x BB), my stack is 38.5 BB, so raising maintains aggression without overcommitting. Consistent with prior opening size of 300 in similar spots from history. Limp or fold too passive; 3x BB raise to 300 is optimal size for value/protection.

I have Q♠8♦ in the small blind facing Elon’s standard open to 300 (pot 450, I must add 250). This is a marginal, out-of-position hand — not strong enough to 3-bet profitably, and folding loses only my small blind. However, the open size is small and Elon is fairly wide/aggressive; calling gives decent pot odds (call 250 into a 450 pot → ~2.8:1) and lets me see a flop with Q-high and some backdoor potential. I’ll call and keep the pot controllable. Plan: fold to a sizable squeeze/3-bet, otherwise proceed cautiously post‑flop — continue with top pair or strong draws, check/fold to heavy pressure with missed boards.

I am in the Big Blind with J2o. Elon (Button) raised to 300, and Minni (Small Blind) called. Although I only need to call 200 more to see a flop in a pot of 700, J2o is an extremely weak hand that performs poorly in multi-way pots and is difficult to play out of position. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I have top pair (Q♠8♦) on a very wet-looking heart board (Q♥5♥4♥). I’m out of position vs Elon (BTN opener) and he’s shown a wide/aggressive raising range historically, which includes two-heart combos that make a flush on this flop. With a mediocre kicker and opponent yet to act, I want to control the pot and keep weaker Qx and bluff-catchers in, while avoiding building a big pot where I’m likely dominated by two hearts or sets. Checking lets me realize equity cheaply and evaluate Elon’s strength. Plan: check and call small-to-medium-sized continuation bets (rough guideline: call ~<=1/2–2/3 pot) for showdown value, but fold to large polarizing overbets/shoves that strongly represent a made flush or a very strong two-pair+ hand. If Elon checks back, I’ll consider a cautious turn bet on safe runouts or a delayed value bet if the turn is a blank.

Flop is 5h Qh 4h, a highly coordinated, monotone board favoring flush draws, wheel straight draws (A3, 23, 36 suited possible), and any heart. My Ad6d is ace-high with no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (gutshot unlikely). However, as preflop raiser from button, my range is wide (Ax, broadway, suited connectors), including some Qx, 5x, 4x, flush draws, which hit this board. Minni's SB call vs BTN raise is wide (suited Ax, connectors, speculative hands), but she checks, suggesting weakness or trapping. With position, deep stacks (mine 35BB effective vs her 97BB), c-bet thin for value against worse Ax/underdraws and protection vs draws. Bet 55% pot (400) balances range, denies equity, builds pot OOP if strong. Checking risks giving free card to draws that beat me; betting exploits her check and maintains aggression consistent with history (frequent postflop leads). Avoid small bet trap; this size polarizes well.

I have top pair (Q♠8♦) but a very vulnerable hand on a two-heart flop (Q♥5♥4♥). Elon opened from the button and now c‑bet 400 into roughly a 700 pot (~57% pot). Against an aggressive, fairly wide button range this is a medium-sized continuation bet that includes a mix of air, weaker Qx, draws and made heart hands. Raising OOP with a mediocre kicker and no backdoor heart is too thin — it bloats the pot and puts me in a bad spot vs many heart combos or sets. Calling keeps his bluffs and worse Qx in, controls the pot size, and allows me to re-evaluate on the turn. Plan: call the 400; fold to a large polarizing shove on the turn (or a big overbet representing the flush/two‑pair), call or raise small turn bets on safe cards, and extract value on blank turn/rivers.

Board: Q♥-5♥-4♥-3♣. I hold Q♠8♦ — top pair with a weak kicker on a three-heart flop. Elon is a wide/aggressive button opener who c-bet the flop; calling kept the pot controllable and allowed me to realize equity. The turn 3♣ is a blank for hearts and doesn’t improve my hand, but I remain very vulnerable to any two-heart combos (which already have the flush) or stronger made hands. Betting out OOP with a marginal kicker is too thin — it bloats the pot and invites raises from hands that dominate me. Checking controls the pot, keeps worse Qx and bluffs in, and lets me call small-to-medium turn bets to reach showdown. Plan: check now. If Elon fires a small/medium bet (roughly ≤ ~pot or ~1/2–1x pot) I will call and re-evaluate the river; if he overbets or shoves polarizing amounts representing a made flush/two‑pair/set I will fold.

Turn board 3c completes my open-ended straight draw (OESD) with Ad6d for 3-4-5-6-x, giving 8 outs to the nuts (2 or 7). Board remains draw-heavy (hearts flush possible, wheel straights, two-pair combos). Minni's check-call flop then check suggests medium strength like Qx, pair + draw, or weaker draw, but not a monster (would lead or raise). As preflop raiser in position with 31BB stack (effective shallower than her 93BB), this is prime semi-bluff spot: high equity (30-40% vs calling range), denies her equity (her flush draws, gutshots), extracts from worse Ax/draws/pairs, and pot-commits me appropriately if called while leaving fold equity. Checking risks free river card improving her (river 2/7 gives me nuts but also completes many draws). Bet 2/3 pot (1000) balances value (overpairs, QT) and bluffs, consistent with aggression history (e.g., Hand 13, 20, 25, 55). Builds pot for EV maximization over 150 hands.

I have top pair (Q♠8♦) with a very weak kicker on a three‑heart board (Q♥5♥4♥ → 3♣). Elon opened from the button and c‑bet the flop; his turn bet of 1000 into a 2500 pot is a small/medium probe (~40% pot) that will include bluffs, worse Qx, and some made heart flushes. Raising OOP with a marginal kicker is too thin — it bloats the pot and plays right into hands that already beat me. Folding now surrenders too much value because I still have decent showdown equity and the bet gives reasonable pot odds. Calling 1000 controls the pot size, keeps worse Qx and bluffs in, and lets me re‑evaluate the river (I will fold to a large river shove that strongly represents the flush or two‑pair/set; call moderate river bets on blanks). Action: call.

I have Q♠8♦ – top pair but a very weak kicker on Q♥-5♥-4♥-3♣-A♠. Elon opened BTN, c‑bet the hearty flop and fired a small turn (line consistent with both made flushes/strong pairs and wide, aggressive bluffs). Pot is 3,500 and Elon’s effective stack is ~2,150; I’m out of position. Betting here only extracts from a narrow subset of worse hands (weaker Qx, some small pairs) while getting called or raised by many hands that already beat me (A‑x, heart flushes, two‑pair/sets).

Given my vulnerable kicker and the wet flop texture, I prefer to keep the pot small and go to showdown. I’ll check the river: if Elon bets a small/medium amount I can call for value, but I’ll fold to a large polarized shove that very strongly represents a made flush/boats. Checking controls the pot and lets me realize my decent showdown equity without bloating the pot into dominated spots.

River As pairs my Ad for top pair (Aces) with a 6 kicker on a board (3c4h5hQhAs) that features a completed heart flush draw, possible wheel straights (A2345), and Qx/two-pair combos. Minni's line (call preflop raise OOP, check-call flop c-bet on monotone board, check-call large turn bet, check river) indicates medium strength like Qx, lower pair + missed draw, or stubborn Ax/weak flush draw, but unlikely a monster (flush/set would raise or lead by now). Her checking range is weak/capping, vulnerable to value bets from my perceived strong range (Ax suited, Qx, sets). With position, 2150 stack (62% pot), and pot 3500, a polarized bet extracts value from worse Ax (A7-AQ), Qx, pairs while folding out missed draws. Betting 1500 (43% pot) sizes well: thin value, fold equity vs bluffs/draws, commits most stack but leaves room if raised (call viable with TPTK blockers). Checking risks conceding pot OOP to her potential Qx/value; aggression consistent with history (e.g., Hands 13,20,25,55 river value) and exploits her passive check-calls over 150-hand game. EV+ bet maximizes stack growth.

I hold top pair Q♠8♦ on Q♥5♥4♥-3♣-A♠ and have already invested on flop and turn. Elon has shown a consistent donk/value line (c-bet flop, bet turn, bet river) but has been wide/aggressive in earlier hands. Calling 1,500 into a 5,000 pot requires ~23% equity (1,500 / (5,000+1,500)), and his small river sizing will include a meaningful number of worse Qx and thin bluffs as well as value (flushes, A‑x that hit on the river, sets). My kicker is weak, so raising would be too thin and would unnecessarily bloat the pot into many hands that beat me. I’ll take the pot odds and call to reach showdown.

On the button versus only the blinds with 7♣3♣: this hand is weak to see a multiway flop, but being on the button gives me position and a good opportunity to steal the blinds. Calling 100 to see a flop is unattractive (I’d need ~40% equity to break even vs two random hands), and 7-3s doesn’t come close to that. A small standard open to 2x the big blind (200 total) gives me fold equity while keeping the pot manageable. If I face a sizable 3-bet I’ll fold — this hand isn’t good enough to defend large vs aggression — but if called I’ll use position to c-bet selectively or fold to strong resistance when I miss. Raise to 200 maximizes EV here by taking advantage of position and fold equity while limiting downside.

In Hand #62, I am in the Small Blind with K7o. Minni, on the button, has raised to 200. This is a relatively small open size (2x). My hand, King-Seven offsuit, is somewhat marginal but has decent high-card value in a 3-handed game. Considering the pot odds (calling 150 to win a total pot that will be 500) and the fact that I am out of position against a button raise, calling is a reasonable defensive play. Folding is also an option if I want to play tight, but against a 2x raise, K7o should be defending a fair amount of the time in the SB. I will call to see a flop and re-evaluate.

AQo is a premium hand from the BB facing a button minraise to 200 and SB flat-call. Button range is wide (many Ax, broadway, suited connectors), SB flatting even wider. AQo has excellent equity against both ranges, dominating many Axo, KQo, etc. 3-betting isolates the button, charges the SB, and builds a pot OOP where we have strong equity. Preflop SPR remains reasonable (~10:1 effective). Sizing to 700 (3.5x the open) is standard with a caller, pressuring weak hands while keeping stacks committed proportionally. Folding or calling is too passive; we want value and fold equity.

I opened to 200 intending to use position, but Elon’s 3-bet to 700 (with Flash already in) turns this into a multiway pot against a strong range. The pot is 1,100 and it costs me 500 to call, so I’d need ~31.25% equity (500 / 1,600) to break even — 7♣3♣ doesn’t have that vs a 3-betting range plus a caller. A raise to 1,200 is a thin 4‑bet and will either commit too many chips or won’t fold out enough of Elon’s range. My preflop plan was to fold to a sizable 3‑bet, so I preserve my stack and wait for a better spot.

I am in the Small Blind with K7o. I initially called Minni's 2x open, but Elon in the Big Blind has 3-bet to 700. Although the price to call is 500 more into a pot of 1,100 (approximately 31% equity needed), K7o is a weak hand to take to a flop against a 3-betting range from the Big Blind. I will be out of position for the rest of the hand and have poor playability. Given that both opponents have around 65 BBs, I would rather wait for a better spot to utilize my chip lead than call a 3-bet with a dominated King.

I am on the button with 7-5 offsuit. This is a very weak starting hand. While it can be tempting to open for a steal on the button, both Elon and Minni have shown a willingness to defend their blinds and play aggressively post-flop. Given the stack sizes and the low potential of my hand, the most disciplined play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop in small blind vs big blind heads-up after button fold. KQo is a strong hand (KQ offsuit ranks in top ~12-15% of hands), well above the range for raising from SB vs BB. Minni's BB defending range will be wide, so we raise for value, build pot, and take initiative. Standard raise size is 3x BB (300). Pot is 150, calling 50 gives great implied odds but limping is weak postflop. Folding is too tight. Effective stacks ~6500-7500 deep enough for playability. History shows Minni calls raises but folds to continued aggression often.

I’m in the big blind with Qs-Jh facing Elon’s open to 300 from the small blind. I’ve already got 100 in; calling requires 200 more for a total commitment of 300. That call gives me decent pot odds (200 to win ~600 → ~25% equity needed) and QJ offsuit has reasonable equity and playability heads-up postflop. Elon opens frequently to 300, so calling keeps weaker hands in and lets me see a flop out of position without committing a large portion of my stack. A minimum 3-bet to 500 is too small to generate fold equity and a larger 3-bet would commit too much with a marginal hand that can be dominated by A/K. I’ll call and evaluate the flop before committing more.

Flop: 3h Qc 2c. I have KQo, flopping top pair top kicker (TPTK) on a draw-heavy board (flush draw with two clubs, wheel/gutshot draws possible with 3-2). Pot 600, acting first heads-up vs Minni's wide BB calling range. History shows Minni calls preflop but often folds to postflop aggression (e.g., Hands 13, 20, 26). C-bet for value from her weaker Qx, Ax, pairs, and to charge/protect against draws. Size ~2/3 pot (400) balances value and fold equity, denies equity, builds pot with strong hand. Stacks deep (72bb/63bb effective), good implied odds if she has draw. Check-fold too passive, check-raise risky OOP. Bet sizing consistent with prior lines (e.g., 350-450). Folding impossible.

I have QsJh on 3h Qc 2c — top pair with a decent but not great kicker, no club. Elon opened pre and c‑bet 400 into the 600 pot (pot is 1000), so calling 400 gives me ~28–29% equity to win a 1400 pot after my call. Against his SB open/c-bet range this is a clear +EV spot to continue: I beat a lot of his missed broadways and pocket pairs and get proper pot odds. Raising to the min (800) OOP would bloat the pot, polarize my range, and often put me in tough spots versus better queens or a reraise. With effective stacks ~6.3k this is a medium SPR — I want to control size and reevaluate on the turn. Plan: call the 400 to realize equity, then fold to heavy turn aggression if a club or Ace arrives and he shows strength; if he checks a blank turn I can take a stab or value-bet thin on later streets.

Heads-up OOP on turn with TPTK (Ks Qd on Qc-3h-2c-8s board). Flop cbet called by Minni's wide BB range (worse Qx like QJ/QT, Ax high, mid pairs 77-TT, stubborn draws like clubs or wheel projects like A4/45). Turn 8s is relatively neutral: bricks clubs flush draw, doesn't complete nut straights (would need 4-5 or A4 already in range), adds some backdoors but favors our made hand. Pot 1400, effective stacks ~5950 (her 5950, us 6850). History vs Minni: calls pre/flop often but folds to turn aggression frequently (e.g., Hand 20 check-call flop then fold river; Hand 26 cbet fold; Hand 51 cbet fold; Hand 55 bet-call flop/turn then check river). Continue thin value betting to charge draws/pairs and exploit her fold equity. Size ~70% pot (1000) balances value from 1-pair hands/draws, builds pot for river, consistent with prior lines (400 flop into ~600 pot). Check risks free river card to 9 outs clubs (~20% equity) or her improving; pot control unnecessary with nuts-ish hand deep. No raise incentive. Deep enough for playability but not overbet jam (~4x, too thin). Betting maximizes EV long-term.

I have Q♠J♥ on 3h Qc 2c 8s — top pair with a medium kicker. Elon opened from the SB, c-bet the flop and now barrels the turn for 1,000 into the ~1,400 pot (pot after the bet ~2,400). Calling 1,000 to win ~2,400 requires ~41.7% equity. Elon opens and barrels fairly frequently, so his turn range contains both value (Qx, KQ, sets, 88) and a meaningful number of missed overcards/club backdoor bluffs. My hand has good showdown value and blocks some Q combos; a small raise to 2,000 would unnecessarily bloat the pot and polarize my range without much fold equity. I’ll call to realize equity, keep worse hands and bluffs in, and reevaluate the river — folding to a clear shove if he further shows strong, unambiguous strength.

River Ad significantly worsens our position: board now Ace-high with TPTK (KQ) downgraded to second pair top kicker. Minni's BB calling range pre/flop/turn includes Ax (now top pair Aces, crushing us), weaker Qx (e.g., QJ/QT), mid-pockets (77-99/JJ?), sets (33/22/QQ unlikely), and stubborn draws (clubs missed, straights impossible, A-wheel like A4 now crushes).
